subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Parts of Speech
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: nouns name people/places/things, verbs show actions, adjectives describe nouns, adverbs describe verbs.

quiz.q: A noun names a person, place, or ___.
Which word is a verb in: The quick dog ran?
Which is an adjective?
Adverbs often tell ___ about a verb.
Which is an adverb?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplication Facts to 10×10
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: students recall multiplication facts to 10×10 fluently and use them to solve problems.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Habitats and Communities
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Life Systems strand: a habitat is where organisms live; a community is all the organisms sharing a habitat and interacting.

quiz.q: A habitat provides food, water, shelter, and ___.
A community includes ___.
options: all living things in an area

quiz.q: Which is a freshwater habitat?
Organisms in a community ___.
options: depend on and interact with each other

quiz.q: A forest habitat includes trees, soil, water, and ___.
options: animals and plants that live there

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Civilizations Overview
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage strand: ancient civilizations developed organized societies with government, art, writing, and technology.

quiz.q: An ancient civilization is ___.
options: a society from thousands of years ago

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT an ancient civilization?
Ancient civilizations contributed ___.
What is archaeology?
Why do we study ancient civilizations?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Sentence Types
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: the four sentence types are declarative (statement), interrogative (question), imperative (command), and exclamatory.

quiz.q: A declarative sentence ___.
An interrogative sentence ends with a ___.
An imperative sentence gives a ___.
An exclamatory sentence expresses ___.
'Close the door!' is a ___ sentence.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Division Basics
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: division is sharing equally or grouping. Students divide with dividends to 100 using multiplication facts.

quiz.q: Division is the inverse (opposite) of ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Food Chains and Webs
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Life Systems: a food chain shows energy flow from producer to consumer. A food web shows interconnected food chains.

quiz.q: Producers in a food chain are ___.
options: plants that make their own food

quiz.q: A herbivore is an animal that eats ___.
In a food web, arrows show ___.
What happens if one species in a food web disappears?
A decomposer ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Egypt
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Ancient Egypt flourished along the Nile River, developing writing (hieroglyphics), monumental architecture, and complex government.

quiz.q: Ancient Egypt was located in ___.
The Nile River was important because ___.
options: it provided soil and water for farming

quiz.q: Egyptian hieroglyphics were ___.
The pyramids were built as ___.
Who ruled Ancient Egypt?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Synonyms and Antonyms
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: synonyms are words with similar meanings; antonyms have opposite meanings. Using them enriches vocabulary and writing.

quiz.q: A synonym for 'happy' is ___.
An antonym for 'ancient' is ___.
Which pair are synonyms?
Which pair are antonyms?
Using synonyms in writing helps to ___.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: 2-Digit × 1-Digit Multiplication
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: multiply 2-digit numbers by 1-digit numbers using the distributive property and place value.

quiz.q: To solve 34 × 5, you can think 30×5 + 4×5 = ?
Which is correct: 58 × 3 = ?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Adaptations
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Life Systems: adaptations are physical or behavioural features that help organisms survive in their habitat.

quiz.q: An adaptation is ___.
options: a feature that helps an organism survive

quiz.q: A polar bear's white fur is an adaptation that helps it ___.
A cactus stores water in its thick stem as an adaptation to ___.
Which is a behavioural adaptation?
Camouflage is an adaptation that helps animals ___.
options: stay hidden from predators or prey

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Greece
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Ancient Greece developed democracy, philosophy, the Olympics, and architecture that influenced the Western world.

quiz.q: Ancient Greece was located in ___.
Democracy was developed in ___.
The Olympic Games began in ___.
Greek philosophers like Socrates were known for ___.
Ancient Greek architecture featured ___.
options: columns and temples like the Parthenon

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Prefixes and Suffixes
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a prefix is added before a root word to change its meaning; a suffix is added after. Examples: un-, re-, -ful, -less.

quiz.q: What does the prefix 'un-' mean?
What does 'unhappy' mean?
What does the suffix '-ful' mean?
Which word means 'without hope'?
What does 'redo' mean?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Introduction to Long Division
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: students divide 2-digit dividends by 1-digit divisors, understanding quotient and remainder.

quiz.q: In division, the number being divided is the ___.
In division, the number dividing is the ___.
27 ÷ 4 = 6 remainder ___.
A remainder in division is ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Biodiversity
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Life Systems: biodiversity means the variety of living organisms in an ecosystem. Greater biodiversity usually means a healthier ecosystem.

quiz.q: Biodiversity means ___.
options: the variety of living organisms in an area

quiz.q: Greater biodiversity usually means ___.
Which habitat has the greatest biodiversity?
How does biodiversity benefit humans?
What threatens biodiversity?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Rome
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Ancient Rome built a vast empire, developing law, engineering, and governance that influenced modern Western civilization.

quiz.q: Ancient Rome was centred in what is now ___.
The Roman Republic had a system of ___.
Roman engineering achievements include ___.
The Roman Empire fell in ___.
What is one way ancient Rome influences us today?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Main Idea and Supporting Details
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Reading strand: the main idea is what a text is mostly about. Supporting details give more information and evidence for the main idea.
subjects.resourceLabel: YouTube: Reading: Main Idea and Supporting Details

quiz.q: The main idea is ___.
options: what the entire text is mostly about

quiz.q: Supporting details ___.
options: explain or prove the main idea
are unrelated to the main idea
are more important than the main idea

quiz.q: Where is the main idea often found?
How do you identify the main idea?
Which is a main idea sentence?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Fractions: Equal Parts
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: a fraction represents equal parts of a whole. The numerator shows how many parts are selected; the denominator shows total equal parts.

quiz.q: In the fraction 3/4, the denominator is ___.
In the fraction 3/4, the numerator is ___.
Which fraction represents half?
A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. You eat 3. What fraction did you eat?
The denominator in a fraction tells you ___.
options: the total number of equal parts

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Human Impact on Ecosystems
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Life Systems: human activities such as deforestation, pollution, and urbanization negatively affect ecosystems; conservation actions help protect them.

quiz.q: Which human activity HARMS ecosystems?
Deforestation means ___.
Which action helps PROTECT ecosystems?
How does pollution affect wildlife?
options: It destroys habitats and harms organisms

quiz.q: What can individuals do to reduce environmental impact?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Indigenous Peoples of North America
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Indigenous peoples of North America include many diverse nations, each with unique cultures, languages, and governance systems.
subjects.resourceLabel: YouTube: Indigenous Peoples of North America

quiz.q: Indigenous peoples of North America ___.
options: have lived here for thousands of years

quiz.q: There are ___ Indigenous cultures in North America.
Traditional Indigenous knowledge includes ___.
Why is it important to learn about Indigenous cultures?
The Indian Act (1876) was ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Inference
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Reading strand: inference means using evidence from the text plus your own knowledge to understand something not directly stated.

quiz.q: An inference is ___.
options: a conclusion from clues and knowledge

quiz.q: What clues help you make an inference?
options: Text evidence plus what you already know

quiz.q: Maria grabbed her umbrella before leaving. You can infer ___.
The book's cover shows a spaceship. You can infer the story is about ___.
After making an inference, a good reader ___.
options: Reads on to check the inference

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing Fractions
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: to compare fractions with the same denominator, compare numerators. To compare different denominators, find equivalent fractions.

quiz.q: Which is greater: 3/5 or 1/5?
Which fraction is closest to 1 (whole)?
Order from least to greatest: 3/8, 1/8, 7/8
Which is the largest fraction?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Rocks and Minerals
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Earth strand: rocks are made of minerals. The three rock types are igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic, each formed by different processes.

quiz.q: The three types of rocks are igneous, sedimentary, and ___.
Igneous rock forms from ___.
Sedimentary rock forms from ___.
options: layers of sediment compressed over time

quiz.q: Metamorphic rock forms when ___.
options: rock changed by heat and pressure

quiz.q: Fossils are most often found in ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Canadian History
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Canada's early history includes Indigenous peoples, European exploration, New France, and British colonization.

quiz.q: Who were the first people to live in what is now Canada?
New France was a colony established by ___.
Jacques Cartier was significant because ___.
options: He first sailed the St. Lawrence River

quiz.q: British and French competition for control of Canada led to ___.
options: conflict including the Seven Years' War

quiz.q: The Battle of the Plains of Abraham (1759) resulted in ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Paragraphs
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Writing strand: a well-organized paragraph has a topic sentence, supporting details, and a concluding sentence. All sentences relate to one main idea.

quiz.q: The topic sentence in a paragraph ___.
options: is unrelated to the other sentences
states the main idea of the paragraph

quiz.q: Supporting sentences in a paragraph ___.
options: support the topic sentence with details

quiz.q: A concluding sentence ___.
How many main ideas should one paragraph have?
Which would make a good topic sentence for a paragraph about dogs?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Adding Fractions with Same Denominator
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: to add fractions with the same denominator, add the numerators and keep the denominator the same.
subjects.resourceLabel: YouTube: Adding Fractions with Same Denominator

quiz.q: When adding fractions with the same denominator, the denominator ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Rock Cycle
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Earth strand: rocks cycle continuously between igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic forms through geological processes.

quiz.q: What drives the rock cycle?
Weathering and erosion break rocks into ___.
Sediment that is compressed and cemented forms ___.
When magma cools it forms ___.
The rock cycle shows that rocks ___.
options: can transform from one type to another

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: European Explorers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: European explorers from the 1400s–1600s sailed to North America, changing the world and affecting Indigenous peoples significantly.

quiz.q: Why did Europeans explore the world in the 1400s–1600s?
options: They wanted to meet Indigenous peoples
They sought new trade routes and resources
They were looking for Canada specifically

quiz.q: Christopher Columbus (1492) sailed for ___.
John Cabot claimed land for ___.
The arrival of Europeans had a ___ impact on Indigenous peoples.
Samuel de Champlain is known for ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Descriptive Writing
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Writing strand: descriptive writing uses sensory details (sight, sound, smell, touch, taste) and vivid language to create a clear picture for the reader.

quiz.q: Descriptive writing uses ___.
Which is a more descriptive sentence?
Sensory details include descriptions of ___.
options: sight, sound, smell, touch, and taste

quiz.q: Vivid adjectives make writing ___.
Which word is more vivid?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Decimals: Tenths
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: a decimal is another way to write a fraction. Tenths represent parts of a whole divided into 10 equal parts (e.g., 0.3 = 3/10).

quiz.q: 0.7 means ___.
Which decimal equals 3/10?
What is 6 tenths as a decimal?
Which is less than 0.5?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Soil Formation
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Earth strand: soil forms over thousands of years by weathering of rock and addition of organic matter. It has layers called horizons.

quiz.q: Soil forms from ___.
The top layer of soil is ___.
Topsoil is important because ___.
options: it is rich in nutrients for plants
it is made of solid rock

quiz.q: How long does it take to form good soil?
What does humus in soil come from?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Government: Democracy and Monarchy
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: governments organize society and make laws. A democracy gives citizens a voice; a monarchy is led by a king or queen.

quiz.q: In a democracy, leaders are ___.
options: chosen by the citizens through voting

quiz.q: Canada is a ___.
Parliament makes ___.
A constitutional monarchy means ___.
options: the monarch's power is limited by law

quiz.q: Voting is important because ___.
options: citizens have a voice in choosing their government

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Persuasive Writing
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Writing strand: persuasive writing argues for a position using reasons, evidence, and a call to action. It has a clear opinion statement.

quiz.q: The purpose of persuasive writing is to ___.
A persuasive text should include ___.
options: a clear opinion, reasons, and evidence

quiz.q: Which is a good opinion statement for a persuasive text?
options: Schools should have longer lunch breaks.

quiz.q: Counter-arguments in persuasive writing ___.
A call to action at the end of a persuasive text asks the reader to ___.
options: do something or change their view

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Decimals: Hundredths
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: hundredths represent parts of a whole divided into 100 equal parts. 0.23 = 23/100 = 2 tenths + 3 hundredths.

quiz.q: Which decimal equals 15/100?
What is 8 hundredths as a decimal?
Which is greater: 0.3 or 0.30?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Properties of Light: Reflection
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: light travels in straight lines and reflects off surfaces. The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection.

quiz.q: Light travels in ___.
Reflection occurs when light ___.
Which surface reflects light best?
The angle of incidence is ___.
options: less than the angle of reflection
greater than the angle of reflection
equal to the angle of reflection

quiz.q: Why does a mirror show your reflection?
options: It reflects light at the same angle

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Trade and Commerce in History
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: trade is the exchange of goods and services. Ancient and early modern civilizations developed trade routes that spread culture and ideas.
subjects.resourceLabel: YouTube: Trade and Commerce in History

quiz.q: Trade is the exchange of ___.
The Silk Road connected ___.
options: Europe, the Middle East, and Asia

quiz.q: Why did civilizations trade with each other?
options: To get goods they couldn't produce

quiz.q: Trade often led to ___.
The fur trade in early Canada involved ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Spelling: ie/ei Words
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: the 'i before e except after c' rule helps spell many words, but has exceptions. Students apply this pattern in context.

quiz.q: Which spelling is correct?
'I before E except after C' means in the word BELIEVE you write ___.
Which is spelled correctly?
After the letter C, you usually write ___ (not ei).
Which word follows the 'i before e except after c' rule?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Money: Making Change
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Number strand: students calculate change by subtracting the price from the amount paid, using coins and bills up to $20.

quiz.q: An item costs $3.75. You pay $5.00. Your change is ___.
An item costs $12.49. You pay $15.00. Your change is ___.
To calculate change, you ___.
options: subtract the price from the amount paid

quiz.q: An item costs $7.89. You pay $10.00. Your change is ___.
You have $20. You buy items for $14.37. Change = ?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Properties of Light: Refraction
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another (e.g., from air to water).

quiz.q: Refraction is the ___ of light.
Refraction occurs when light passes from one ___ to another.
A straw in a glass of water appears to be bent because of ___.
A prism separates white light into its colours because different colours ___ at different angles.
A rainbow is an example of ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Human Rights
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: human rights are basic rights all people deserve. The Universal Declaration of Human Rights (UDHR, 1948) protects these rights globally.

quiz.q: Human rights are rights that ___.
The Universal Declaration of Human Rights was adopted in ___.
Which is a basic human right?
Who is responsible for upholding human rights?
Why are human rights important?
options: They only apply in certain countries

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Adverbs
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: adverbs modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs. They tell how, when, where, or to what degree something happens.

quiz.q: An adverb modifies a ___.
Which word is an adverb in: She spoke softly?
Adverbs of manner tell ___.
Adverbs of time tell ___.
Many adverbs end in ___.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Patterns: Numeric and Geometric
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Algebra and Patterning strand: numeric patterns follow a mathematical rule; geometric patterns involve shapes. Students identify rules and extend patterns.

quiz.q: What is the rule? 3, 6, 9, 12...
What comes next? 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, ___
A geometric pattern uses ___.
What is the rule? 100, 90, 80, 70...
Which is an example of a geometric pattern?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Sound: Vibration and Frequency
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: sounds are made by vibrations. Frequency determines pitch — higher frequency = higher pitch. Amplitude determines volume.

quiz.q: Sound is caused by ___.
Frequency refers to ___.
A high-frequency sound has a ___ pitch.
Amplitude of a sound wave determines its ___.
In which medium does sound travel fastest?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canadian Geography: Provinces and Territories
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Canada has 10 provinces and 3 territories. Each has a capital city and distinct geographic features.
subjects.resourceLabel: YouTube: Canadian Geography: Provinces and Territories

quiz.q: How many provinces does Canada have?
How many territories does Canada have?
What is the largest province in Canada by area?
What is Canada's capital city?
Which territory is the newest, created in 1999?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Context Clues
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: context clues are words and phrases near an unknown word that help you determine its meaning without a dictionary.

quiz.q: Context clues are ___.
options: words nearby that hint at meaning

quiz.q: Which type of context clue gives a definition within the text?
Using context clues helps readers ___.
options: look up every word in a dictionary

quiz.q: In 'The magnanimous king gave generously to the poor,' magnanimous means ___.
Which strategy helps most with a word you do not know?
options: Read the surrounding sentences for clues

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Geometry: Angles
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Geometry strand: an angle is formed by two rays with a common endpoint. A right angle = 90°, acute angle < 90°, obtuse angle > 90°.

quiz.q: A right angle measures exactly ___.
An acute angle measures ___.
An obtuse angle measures ___.
A straight angle measures ___.
Which tool measures angles?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Pulleys and Gears
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: pulleys and gears are simple machines that redirect or change force. Gears can speed up or slow down motion and change direction.

quiz.q: A pulley is used to ___.
options: lift or move loads by redirecting force

quiz.q: In a fixed pulley, the force needed to lift a load ___.
A moveable pulley ___ the force needed.
When two gears mesh, they rotate in ___ directions.
A larger gear turning a smaller gear makes the smaller gear rotate ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ontario Geography
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Ontario is Canada's most populous province with diverse geography including the Great Lakes, Canadian Shield, and Great Lakes–St. Lawrence Lowlands.

quiz.q: Ontario borders how many of the Great Lakes?
The Canadian Shield in Ontario is characterized by ___.
The most populous city in Ontario is ___.
Ontario's provincial capital is ___.
Which region of Ontario is most suited for farming?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Compare and Contrast
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Reading strand: comparing shows how two things are alike; contrasting shows how they differ. Signal words: both, similarly, however, but, in contrast.

quiz.q: Comparing means finding how things are ___.
Contrasting means finding how things are ___.
Which signal word shows a contrast?
A Venn diagram helps ___.
options: compare and contrast two or more things

quiz.q: Which text type often uses comparison and contrast?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Area and Perimeter
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Measurement strand: perimeter is the distance around a shape; area is the space inside. Area of a rectangle = length × width; perimeter = 2(l + w).

quiz.q: Perimeter is the ___.
options: distance around the outside of a shape

quiz.q: Area is measured in ___.
A rectangle is 5 cm long and 3 cm wide. Area = ?
A rectangle is 6 cm long and 4 cm wide. Perimeter = ?
Area of a square with side 7 cm = ?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Electricity: Static and Current
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: static electricity is a build-up of charge; current electricity is a flow of electrons through a conductor.

quiz.q: Static electricity is caused by ___.
options: a flow of electrons in a circuit

quiz.q: Current electricity flows through ___.
Which is a good conductor of electricity?
An insulator ___.
Which is an example of static electricity?
options: A lightning bolt or a rubbed balloon

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Maps: Physical Features
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: physical maps show natural features like mountains, rivers, lakes, and plains. They use colour and symbols to represent terrain.

quiz.q: A physical map shows ___.
On most physical maps, blue represents ___.
What is a legend (key) on a map?
options: A table explaining the map's symbols

quiz.q: Which tool on a map shows distance?
The Rocky Mountains in Canada are located in ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Similes
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a simile compares two unlike things using 'like' or 'as'. Example: 'brave as a lion,' 'runs like the wind'.

quiz.q: A simile compares two things using ___.
Which is a simile?
What does 'brave as a lion' mean?
Similes make writing more ___.
Which is NOT a simile?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Metric Measurement: Length
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Measurement strand: the metric system uses kilometres (km), metres (m), centimetres (cm), and millimetres (mm). 1 m = 100 cm; 1 km = 1000 m.

quiz.q: 1 metre = ___ centimetres.
1 kilometre = ___ metres.
Which unit would you use to measure the length of a pencil?
Which unit would you use to measure the distance between two cities?
Convert 3 m to cm.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Electric Circuits
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: a complete circuit allows electricity to flow. It needs a power source, conductors, and a load (device). Circuits can be series or parallel.

quiz.q: For electricity to flow, a circuit must be ___.
Which is NOT needed in a basic circuit?
In a series circuit, components are connected ___.
options: one after another in one loop

quiz.q: In a parallel circuit, each branch has ___.
options: the same current as all others
its own direct path to the power source

quiz.q: If a bulb in a series circuit burns out, the other bulbs ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada's Natural Resources
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: Canada is rich in natural resources including forests, minerals, freshwater, oil, and agricultural land. These support the economy but must be managed sustainably.

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT a natural resource?
Why are Canada's forests valuable?
options: Lumber, paper, habitat, and climate help

quiz.q: What energy resource does Alberta have in large quantities?
Sustainable use of resources means ___.
options: only using what you can carry
using them as fast as possible

quiz.q: Which province is known for potash mining?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Metaphors
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a metaphor directly states that one thing IS another unlike thing, without using 'like' or 'as'. Example: 'Life is a journey.'

quiz.q: A metaphor says one thing ___ another.
Which is a metaphor?
options: He is as strong as an ox.

quiz.q: What does 'Time is money' mean?
options: Time is valuable, don't waste it

quiz.q: A metaphor differs from a simile because it ___.
options: directly states one thing is another

quiz.q: Which is a metaphor?
options: Her voice was music to my ears.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Mass and Volume Measurement
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Measurement strand: mass is measured in grams (g) and kilograms (kg). Volume is measured in litres (L) and millilitres (mL). 1 kg = 1000 g; 1 L = 1000 mL.

quiz.q: 1 kilogram = ___ grams.
1 litre = ___ millilitres.
Which unit measures how heavy something is?
Which unit measures the amount of liquid in a bottle?
A bag weighs 2500 g. How many kilograms is that?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Forces: Gravity and Friction
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Energy strand: gravity is the force that pulls objects toward Earth. Friction is the force that resists movement between surfaces in contact.

quiz.q: Gravity is a force that ___.
Friction is a force that ___.
Which surface creates MORE friction?
Friction produces ___.
Without gravity, objects on Earth would ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Environment: Conservation
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies: conservation means protecting and managing natural resources wisely to prevent their depletion and preserve environments for future generations.

quiz.q: Conservation means ___.
Which is an example of conservation?
Canada Day (July 1) celebrates ___.
Why should we conserve freshwater?
options: Only 3% of Earth's water is fresh

quiz.q: Which action MOST helps conserve forests?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Sequencing
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Reading strand: sequence is the order in which events happen. Signal words: first, then, next, after, finally, before.

quiz.q: Sequence means ___.
options: the order in which events occur

quiz.q: Which signal word shows a sequence?
Why is sequence important in a how-to text?
options: All steps can be done in any order

quiz.q: Which goes last in a story's sequence?
'First mix the batter, then bake it' shows ___.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Data: Bar Graphs
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Data strand: a bar graph uses horizontal or vertical bars to compare quantities across categories. Students read, create, and interpret bar graphs.

quiz.q: A bar graph is used to ___.
The vertical axis of a bar graph usually shows ___.
If the 'Dogs' bar reaches 12 and 'Cats' bar reaches 8, there are ___ more dogs.
What is the title of a graph used for?
options: To tell what data the graph shows

quiz.q: A bar graph differs from a pictograph because ___.
options: bar graphs use bars instead of pictures/symbols

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Properties of Liquids and Solids
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science Materials strand: solids have definite shape and volume; liquids have definite volume but take the shape of their container.

subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: rounding numbers to the nearest ten, hundred, or thousand makes estimating sums, differences, and totals faster and easier to check for reasonableness.

quiz.q: Round 3,482 to the nearest thousand.
Round 6,251 to the nearest hundred.
Estimate the sum: 2,890 + 3,150 is approximately ___.
Why is estimating useful before solving a problem exactly?
options: It replaces the need to solve the problem
It has no real use in math
It helps you check whether your final answer is reasonable
It always gives the exact answer

quiz.q: Round 749 to the nearest hundred.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Species at Risk in Canadian Ecosystems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Life Systems strand: species at risk are plants or animals in Canada facing possible extinction, often due to habitat loss, pollution, or climate change, and conservation programs work to protect them.

quiz.q: A species at risk is one that is ___.
options: No longer affected by its environment

quiz.q: Which is a common cause of species becoming at risk?
options: Too many predators being removed by nature naturally

quiz.q: Which Canadian animal has historically been considered at risk?
Conservation programs for species at risk often involve ___.
Why do scientists track species at risk closely?
options: Only endangered plants are ever tracked
To take action before a species disappears entirely

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Mesopotamia
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Ancient Mesopotamia, located between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers, is considered one of the earliest civilizations, known for early writing, cities, and irrigation systems.

quiz.q: Ancient Mesopotamia developed between which two rivers?
Mesopotamia is often called one of the earliest ___.
Which early writing system developed in Mesopotamia?
Irrigation systems in Mesopotamia were used mainly to ___.
options: Bring river water to farmland for growing crops

quiz.q: Why did early civilizations like Mesopotamia often develop near rivers?
options: Rivers provided water for farming, drinking, and transportation
Rivers were avoided by early people

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Comparative and Superlative Adjectives
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: comparative adjectives (bigger, faster) compare two things, while superlative adjectives (biggest, fastest) compare three or more things, often adding -er or -est.

quiz.q: Which is the comparative form of tall?
Which is the superlative form of fast?
Comparative adjectives are used to compare ___.
Superlative adjectives are used to compare ___.
Choose the correct sentence: This is the ___ mountain in the range.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplying by 10, 100, and 1,000
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: multiplying a whole number by 10, 100, or 1,000 shifts its digits left, adding that many zeros, such as 25 times 100 equals 2,500.

quiz.q: What is 34 multiplied by 10?
What is 56 multiplied by 100?
What is 7 multiplied by 1,000?
Multiplying by 100 is the same as adding how many zeros?
What is 120 multiplied by 10?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Changes of State: Melting, Freezing, and Evaporating
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: matter changes state through processes like melting (solid to liquid), freezing (liquid to solid), and evaporating (liquid to gas), usually caused by adding or removing heat.

quiz.q: Melting occurs when a solid changes into a ___ after gaining heat.
Freezing occurs when a liquid changes into a solid after ___ heat.
Evaporation occurs when a liquid changes into a ___.
Which is an everyday example of melting?
options: An ice cube turning into water on a warm day

quiz.q: What generally causes a substance to change state?
options: Nothing can cause a change of state

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient China
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Ancient China developed along the Yellow and Yangtze rivers, and is known for early inventions such as paper, silk production, and the Great Wall.

quiz.q: Ancient China developed along which rivers?
Which invention is credited to Ancient China?
Silk production was an important early industry in ___.
The Great Wall of China was built mainly to ___.
Why do historians study Ancient China's early inventions?
options: They influenced technology and trade for centuries afterward
Only modern inventions matter to historians

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Formal and Informal Letters
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: formal letters use polite, professional language for serious purposes (like a business), while informal letters use casual, friendly language for people you know well.

quiz.q: A formal letter is typically used for ___.
options: Writing to a close friend casually

quiz.q: Which greeting fits a formal letter?
An informal letter is best suited for ___.
options: A formal complaint to a company
Writing to a close friend or family member

quiz.q: Which closing best fits a formal letter?
Why does letter tone change between formal and informal writing?
options: All letters should sound the same
To match the purpose and relationship with the reader

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Mixed Numbers and Improper Fractions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: a mixed number combines a whole number and a fraction (like 2 and 1/2), while an improper fraction has a numerator larger than its denominator (like 5/2), and the two can be converted between each other.

quiz.q: Which is a mixed number?
Which is an improper fraction?
Convert the mixed number 1 and 3/4 to an improper fraction.
Convert the improper fraction 9/4 to a mixed number.
In an improper fraction, the numerator is ___ the denominator.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Simple Machines: Levers and Inclined Planes
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: a lever uses a pivot point (fulcrum) to lift or move objects with less effort, while an inclined plane is a sloped surface that makes raising objects easier.

quiz.q: A lever uses a ___ to help lift or move objects.
An inclined plane is best described as a ___.
Which is an everyday example of an inclined plane?
A seesaw is an example of which simple machine?
Why do inclined planes make lifting heavy objects easier?
options: They spread the effort over a longer distance instead of lifting straight up

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Daily Life and Social Roles
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: early societies such as Egypt, Greece, Rome, Mesopotamia, and China had structured daily lives, with different social roles for rulers, workers, farmers, and families.

quiz.q: In many early societies, social roles were often organized by ___.
options: Class or status, such as rulers, workers, and farmers
Age only, with no other factors

quiz.q: Farmers in early societies were important because they ___.
options: Produced food to support the population
Had no role in daily life

quiz.q: Rulers in early civilizations often held responsibility for ___.
options: Leading the society and making major decisions

quiz.q: Studying daily life in early societies helps us understand ___.
options: How people lived, worked, and organized their communities long ago

quiz.q: Which role would likely have existed in most early societies?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Idioms
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: an idiom is a phrase whose meaning cannot be understood from the literal words alone, such as break the ice or it is raining cats and dogs.

quiz.q: An idiom is a phrase whose meaning ___.
options: Cannot be understood from its literal words alone

quiz.q: What does the idiom break the ice mean?
options: To ease tension and start a conversation

quiz.q: What does it is raining cats and dogs mean?
options: Animals are falling from the sky

quiz.q: Why can idioms be confusing for new readers?
options: They only exist in one language
Their meaning differs from what the individual words literally say
They are always easy to understand literally

quiz.q: Which is an example of an idiom?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Adding and Subtracting Decimals
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: adding and subtracting decimals requires lining up the decimal points so digits with the same place value are combined correctly.

quiz.q: What is 6.80 minus 2.30?
When adding decimals, it is important to ___.
What is 10.00 minus 4.35?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Structures: Load and Support
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: a load is the weight a structure must support, and good structural design distributes that load evenly to prevent collapse.

quiz.q: A load on a structure refers to ___.
options: The weight the structure must support

quiz.q: Why is it important for a structure to distribute load evenly?
options: It makes the structure heavier for no reason
It only matters for small structures
To prevent weak points that could cause collapse

quiz.q: Which structural feature helps support heavy loads?
A bridge is designed to safely support the load of ___.
options: Vehicles, people, and its own weight
Only itself with nothing on it

quiz.q: What might happen if a structure's support cannot handle its load?
options: The structure could weaken or collapse

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Beliefs and Traditions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: early societies such as Egypt, Greece, Rome, Mesopotamia, and China developed belief systems, gods, and traditions that shaped their culture, art, and daily practices.

quiz.q: Many early societies developed belief systems that included ___.
Beliefs and traditions in early societies often influenced ___.
Why do historians study the belief systems of early societies?
options: Only written laws matter to historians
To better understand their culture, values, and way of life
Beliefs have no connection to culture

quiz.q: Which is an example of how belief systems shaped early societies?
options: Building temples and monuments for religious purposes
All societies built the same structures for the same reasons
Beliefs had no effect on architecture

quiz.q: Comparing beliefs across early societies shows that ___.
options: Only one early society had any beliefs
Different societies expressed traditions in unique but sometimes similar ways
All beliefs were exactly identical worldwide

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Making Predictions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: making predictions means using clues from the title, pictures, and text so far to thoughtfully guess what might happen next in a story.

quiz.q: Making a prediction while reading means ___.
options: Using clues from the text to thoughtfully guess what happens next
Skipping to the end of the book

quiz.q: Which is a good source of clues for making predictions?
Why do good readers make predictions while reading?
options: It only works for non-fiction texts
Predictions are not useful for reading
It keeps them thinking actively and engaged with the text
It replaces the need to read the rest of the story

quiz.q: If a story's cover shows dark clouds and a worried character, you might predict ___.
options: Something tense or stormy may happen
Nothing can be predicted from a cover

quiz.q: After making a prediction, good readers should ___.
options: Check whether the story confirms or changes their prediction

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Classifying Triangles
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Geometry strand: triangles can be classified by their sides (equilateral, isosceles, scalene) and by their angles (right, acute, obtuse).

quiz.q: A triangle with all three sides equal is called ___.
A triangle with exactly two equal sides is called ___.
A triangle with no equal sides is called ___.
A triangle with one 90-degree angle is called a ___ triangle.
A triangle with all angles less than 90 degrees is called ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Space: Constellations and the Night Sky
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: constellations are patterns of stars that people have named and used for navigation and storytelling, appearing to shift position across the seasons.

quiz.q: A constellation is a ___.
options: Pattern of stars that has been named

quiz.q: Constellations have historically been used for ___.
Which is a well-known constellation visible from Canada?
Why do the visible constellations change with the seasons?
options: The sky is different every night randomly
Earth's position in orbit around the Sun changes our view of the night sky

quiz.q: Constellations are best observed ___.
options: On a clear night away from bright city lights

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparing Physical Regions of Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada includes diverse physical regions, such as the Canadian Shield, the Prairies, the Rocky Mountains, and coastal areas, each with distinct landforms and climates.

quiz.q: Which physical region of Canada is known for flat, fertile farmland?
The Canadian Shield is mainly made up of ___.
Which region features tall, rugged mountains in western Canada?
Why do Canada's physical regions have different climates and landscapes?
options: Climate has no connection to landforms
Canada has only one physical region
All regions of Canada have identical climates
Their location, landforms, and distance from oceans affect their climate

quiz.q: Comparing physical regions helps students understand ___.
options: How geography shapes where and how people live
That geography has no effect on communities
That climate is the same everywhere in Canada

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Homophones
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Language strand: homophones are words that sound the same but have different spellings and meanings, such as their/there/they are, or to/too/two.

quiz.q: Which set of words are homophones?
Choose the correct homophone: ___ going to the park.
Choose the correct homophone: I left my book over ___.
Homophones are words that ___.
options: Always mean the exact same thing
Sound the same but have different spellings and meanings
Are spelled the same but sound different

quiz.q: Which is the correct use: I have ___ apples.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Introducing Quadrilaterals
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Geometry strand: a quadrilateral is any shape with four sides, including squares, rectangles, rhombuses, and trapezoids, each with their own specific properties.

quiz.q: A quadrilateral always has how many sides?
Which shape is a quadrilateral with four equal sides and four right angles?
A rectangle has ___ pairs of equal opposite sides.
A trapezoid has exactly how many parallel sides?
Which of these is NOT a quadrilateral?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Weather Instruments and Measurement
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: scientists use instruments such as thermometers, rain gauges, and wind vanes to measure and track weather conditions like temperature, precipitation, and wind direction.

quiz.q: A thermometer is used to measure ___.
A rain gauge is used to measure ___.
A wind vane is used to determine ___.
Why do meteorologists use weather instruments?
options: To accurately measure and track weather conditions
Instruments provide no useful weather data
To guess randomly instead of measuring

quiz.q: Which instrument would help you know if a storm brought a lot of rain?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Indigenous Peoples: Contributions to Canadian Society
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Indigenous peoples have made significant and lasting contributions to Canadian society, including knowledge of the land, agriculture, medicine, governance, and culture.

quiz.q: Indigenous peoples have contributed knowledge about ___ to Canadian society.
Which crop, first cultivated by Indigenous peoples, remains important today?
Indigenous governance systems have influenced ideas about ___.
options: Only ancient practices with no modern relevance

quiz.q: Why is it important to recognize Indigenous contributions to Canada?
options: It provides a fuller, more accurate understanding of Canadian history and culture
Canadian history began only with European settlers

quiz.q: Indigenous art, storytelling, and traditions continue to ___ Canadian culture today.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Poetry
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: poetry uses rhythm, imagery, and sometimes rhyme to express ideas and feelings in a creative, condensed form, often organized into lines and stanzas.

quiz.q: A stanza in a poem is similar to a ___ in prose writing.
Poetry often uses ___ to create vivid mental pictures for the reader.
Rhyme in poetry means ___.
options: Words with the same ending sound

quiz.q: Which is a common feature of poetry?
options: Rhythm and word choice for effect

quiz.q: Why might a poet choose poetry instead of a regular paragraph to express an idea?
options: To use rhythm, imagery, and condensed language for creative effect
There is no reason to choose poetry
Poetry cannot express any real ideas
Paragraphs are not allowed to express feelings

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Factors and Multiples
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: a factor is a number that divides evenly into another number, while a multiple is the result of multiplying a number by a whole number.

quiz.q: Which is a factor of 12?
Which is a multiple of 6?
A factor of a number ___ evenly into it.
List the factors of 10.
The first three multiples of 7 are ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Sound: How Sound Travels Through Materials
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: sound is a vibration that travels through different materials -- solids, liquids, and gases -- typically fastest through solids and slowest through gases like air.

quiz.q: Sound travels as a type of ___.
Sound generally travels fastest through ___.
options: A vacuum with nothing in it

quiz.q: Sound cannot travel through ___.
Why can you sometimes hear sound better with your ear against a solid surface, like a table?
options: Sound travels efficiently through solid materials
It has nothing to do with vibrations

quiz.q: Which best describes how sound moves through air to reach our ears?
options: As vibrating air particles carrying the sound wave
Sound does not move at all

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada's Relationships with Other Countries
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada maintains relationships with other countries through trade, diplomacy, and international cooperation, which affect its economy and global connections.

quiz.q: Trade with other countries allows Canada to ___.
options: Stop producing its own goods entirely
Only sell goods, never buy any
Exchange goods and services with the rest of the world

quiz.q: Diplomacy between countries generally involves ___.
Which is an example of an item Canada might export to other countries?
options: Items made exclusively in other countries
Natural resources like lumber or minerals

quiz.q: Why are international relationships important for Canada's economy?
options: International relationships have no economic effect
They create trade opportunities that support jobs and economic growth
Trade only benefits other countries, never Canada
Canada's economy operates in complete isolation

quiz.q: International cooperation between countries can help address ___.
options: Problems within a single country only
Shared challenges, such as environmental or economic issues

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Advertising Techniques
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Media Literacy strand: advertisers use techniques such as emotional appeals, celebrity endorsements, and repetition to persuade audiences to buy products or believe messages.

quiz.q: An emotional appeal in advertising tries to ___.
options: Persuade by triggering feelings like excitement or happiness
Avoid influencing the audience at all

quiz.q: A celebrity endorsement in an ad means ___.
options: A famous person promotes the product
Only scientists appear in the ad
The ad contains no persuasive elements
The product has no advertising at all

quiz.q: Repetition in advertising is used to ___.
options: Help the audience remember the brand or message
Slow down the ad's pacing for no reason

quiz.q: Why should viewers think critically about advertising techniques?
options: Critical thinking is unnecessary for ads
Advertising has no techniques worth noticing
To recognize persuasion methods and make informed decisions
All ads present only unbiased facts

quiz.q: Which is an example of an advertising technique?
options: Using a catchy jingle or slogan
Providing no information about the product

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Patterning: Input-Output Tables
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Algebra strand: an input-output table shows how a rule transforms an input number into an output number, helping students identify and apply numeric rules.

quiz.q: If the rule is add 5, what is the output when the input is 3?
If the rule is multiply by 2, what is the output when the input is 6?
In an input-output table, the input is ___.
options: The starting number before applying the rule

quiz.q: If input 4 gives output 12, and input 5 gives output 15, what is the rule?
Input-output tables are useful because they help you ___.
options: Identify and apply a consistent numeric rule

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Light: Absorption and Colour
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: objects appear a certain colour based on which wavelengths of light they reflect and which they absorb; a black object absorbs most light, while a white object reflects most light.

quiz.q: A black object appears black because it ___ most light.
A white object appears white because it ___ most light.
An object appears a certain colour based on which light wavelengths it ___.
On a sunny day, why might a black shirt feel hotter than a white shirt?
options: Colour has no effect on heat absorption
Black absorbs more light energy, which converts to heat
White absorbs more heat than black
Black reflects all light energy away

quiz.q: If an object reflects only green light and absorbs the rest, it will appear ___.

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Landforms and Bodies of Water in Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada contains diverse landforms and bodies of water, including mountains, plains, rivers, and lakes, such as the Great Lakes and the St. Lawrence River.

quiz.q: Which are the five connected lakes on the Canada-United States border?
The St. Lawrence River is important because it ___.
options: Is located entirely in another country
Connects the Great Lakes to the Atlantic Ocean, supporting trade and travel

quiz.q: Which landform is a large, flat area often used for farming?
Studying Canada's landforms and water bodies helps us understand ___.
options: That all of Canada looks the same
That Canada has no significant water bodies
How geography influences where communities settle and how they live
That geography has no effect on daily life

quiz.q: Which is an example of a landform found in Canada?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Quotation Marks and Dialogue
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: quotation marks show the exact words a person says in dialogue, with punctuation like commas and periods placed carefully to show who is speaking.

quiz.q: Quotation marks are used to show ___.
Which sentence correctly uses quotation marks?
options: “Maria said I am ready to go
Maria said I am ready to go.
Maria said, “I am ready to go.”
Maria said, I am ready to go.

quiz.q: Dialogue in a story refers to ___.
In dialogue, a comma is often placed ___.
options: Before the closing quotation mark, before the speaker tag
Only at the end of the whole story

quiz.q: Why is dialogue useful in storytelling?
options: It shows characters' personalities and moves the plot forward through their speech
Dialogue has no effect on a story
It is only used in non-fiction
It replaces the need for a plot

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multi-Step Word Problems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: multi-step word problems require identifying and completing more than one operation in the correct order to reach a final answer.

quiz.q: A store had 240 toys, sold 85, then received a shipment of 60 more. How many toys now?
Liam saved $45, spent $18, then earned $12 more. How much does Liam have now?
A school has 4 classes of 25 students each, and 10 students are absent today. How many are present?
Solving a multi-step problem requires you to ___.
options: Use only one operation and ignore the rest
Complete each necessary step in the correct order

quiz.q: A baker made 150 muffins, sold 3 dozen, then baked 24 more. How many muffins are there now?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Electricity: Conductors and Insulators
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: conductors, like most metals, allow electricity to flow through them easily, while insulators, like rubber and plastic, resist the flow of electricity and are used for safety.

quiz.q: A conductor is a material that ___.
options: Allows electricity to flow through it easily

quiz.q: An insulator is a material that ___.
Which material is typically a good conductor?
Why are electrical wires often covered in rubber or plastic?
options: To insulate them and prevent dangerous electric shocks
Covering wires has no safety purpose
To make the wires conduct more electricity

quiz.q: Which of these is typically used as an insulator?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: How Canada's Provinces and Territories Formed
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada's provinces and territories joined the country at different times in history, starting with Confederation in 1867 and continuing as new provinces and territories were added.

quiz.q: In what year did Confederation create the original Dominion of Canada?
Canada's provinces and territories joined the country ___.
options: All at exactly the same time

quiz.q: How many provinces and territories make up Canada today?
Why is it useful to learn how Canada's provinces and territories formed?
options: It helps explain Canada's history and how the country grew over time
It has no connection to understanding Canada today
Provinces have always existed exactly as they are now
This history has no educational value

quiz.q: Confederation in 1867 originally united which regions?
options: Ontario, Quebec, Nova Scotia, and New Brunswick
All ten current provinces at once

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Determining Importance
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: determining importance means identifying which details in a text are essential to understanding it, separating key information from interesting but less important details.

quiz.q: Determining importance while reading means ___.
options: Identifying which details are most essential to understanding the text

quiz.q: Which is more likely to be an essential detail in an informational text?
options: The font used in the book
A key fact directly supporting the main idea

quiz.q: Why is determining importance a useful reading strategy?
options: It replaces the need to read carefully
It helps readers focus on and remember the most meaningful information
It means ignoring the main idea
It has no benefit while reading

quiz.q: When summarizing a text, determining importance helps you ___.
options: Choose only the most essential information to include
Include every single detail from the text

quiz.q: Which reading skill is closely related to determining importance?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Coordinate Grids: Plotting Points
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Geometry strand: a coordinate grid uses a horizontal x-axis and vertical y-axis to locate points using ordered pairs, such as (3, 2), which means 3 units across and 2 units up.

quiz.q: In the ordered pair (3, 2), which number represents the horizontal position?
The horizontal axis on a coordinate grid is called the ___.
The point where the x-axis and y-axis meet is called the ___.
To plot the point (4, 1), you would move ___.
options: Nowhere, since the point does not exist

quiz.q: An ordered pair like (2, 5) is written in the order ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Erosion and Its Effects on Landforms
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: erosion is the gradual wearing away and movement of soil and rock by wind, water, or ice, slowly shaping landforms like valleys and canyons over long periods of time.

quiz.q: Erosion is best described as the ___.
options: Gradual wearing away and movement of soil and rock

quiz.q: Which of these can cause erosion?
A river slowly carving out a canyon over time is an example of ___.
Erosion typically occurs ___.
options: Gradually, often over long periods of time

quiz.q: Why might people plant vegetation along a riverbank?
options: Vegetation has no effect on erosion
Plant roots can help hold soil in place and reduce erosion

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: My Role as a Canadian Citizen
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: being a Canadian citizen involves rights, such as voting when old enough, and responsibilities, such as respecting laws, the environment, and the wellbeing of the community.

quiz.q: A responsibility of Canadian citizens includes ___.
options: Respecting laws and contributing to the community

quiz.q: Once old enough, Canadian citizens gain the right to ___.
options: Avoid paying any attention to government

quiz.q: Why might a student get involved in a local community project?
options: To practice being an active, responsible citizen
It is required to skip school
Only adults can contribute to communities

quiz.q: Respecting the environment is considered part of good citizenship because ___.
options: Protecting shared resources benefits the whole community
The environment has no connection to citizenship
Only government workers must consider the environment
It has no lasting impact on anyone

quiz.q: Reflecting on your role as a citizen helps you understand ___.
options: That citizenship applies only to adults
How your actions can positively contribute to your community and country
That being a citizen has no responsibilities

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Point of View
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: point of view is the perspective from which a story is told, either first person (using I or we) or third person (using he, she, or they).

quiz.q: A story told using I or we is written in ___ point of view.
A story told using he, she, or they is written in ___ point of view.
Point of view refers to ___.
options: The font used in a book
The perspective from which a story is told

quiz.q: Why does point of view matter to a reader?
options: It shapes what information and feelings the reader has access to
It has no effect on how a story is understood
Point of view never changes a story
It only affects the book’s cover

quiz.q: Which pronoun signals first-person point of view?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Division with Remainders
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: when a number does not divide evenly, the amount left over is called the remainder, written as R after the quotient, such as 17 divided by 5 equals 3 remainder 2.

quiz.q: What is 17 divided by 5?
A remainder is ___.
options: The amount left over after dividing evenly

quiz.q: What is 23 divided by 4?
If a division problem has no remainder, the numbers divide ___.
What is 29 divided by 6?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Life Cycles of Animals
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Life Systems strand: many animals go through distinct life cycle stages, such as egg, larva, pupa, and adult for insects, or birth, growth, and maturity for mammals.

quiz.q: Which is a typical life cycle stage for many insects?
options: Egg and adult only, with nothing between
Only adult, with no other stages

quiz.q: A caterpillar is which stage in a butterfly’s life cycle?
Mammals typically go through stages including ___.
Why is studying life cycles useful in science?
options: Life cycles have no scientific value
It helps explain how living things grow, change, and reproduce over time
All animals have identical life cycles

quiz.q: The pupa stage in an insect’s life cycle is when ___.
options: The insect transforms into its adult form
The insect is first laid as an egg

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient India
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Ancient India developed along the Indus River, and is known for early city planning, a written script, and significant early achievements in mathematics.

quiz.q: Ancient India developed along which river?
Ancient Indian cities were known for early examples of ___.
Which subject did Ancient India make significant early contributions to?
Why do historians consider Ancient India an early major civilization?
options: It developed advanced cities, writing, and knowledge systems early in history
It was identical to every other ancient civilization

quiz.q: Studying Ancient India alongside Egypt, China, and Mesopotamia helps us ___.
options: Prove that only one civilization ever existed
Compare how different early civilizations developed

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Irregular Verbs
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: irregular verbs do not follow the usual pattern of adding -ed for the past tense, such as go becoming went or eat becoming ate.

quiz.q: What is the past tense of the irregular verb go?
What is the past tense of the irregular verb eat?
An irregular verb is one that ___.
options: Does not follow the usual -ed pattern for the past tense
Always adds -ed for the past tense

quiz.q: What is the past tense of the irregular verb see?
Which of these is an irregular verb?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Subtracting Fractions with the Same Denominator
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: to subtract fractions with the same denominator, subtract the numerators and keep the denominator the same, such as 5/8 minus 2/8 equals 3/8.

quiz.q: What is 5/8 minus 2/8?
What is 7/10 minus 4/10?
When subtracting fractions with the same denominator, you ___.
options: Subtract the numerators and keep the denominator the same

quiz.q: What is 9/12 minus 5/12?
What is 6/9 minus 6/9?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Classifying Materials: Natural and Synthetic
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: natural materials come directly from nature, such as wood or cotton, while synthetic materials are human-made, such as plastic or nylon.

quiz.q: A natural material is one that ___.
options: Is entirely human-made in a factory
Does not exist in the real world

quiz.q: A synthetic material is one that is ___.
Which of these is a natural material?
Which of these is a synthetic material?
Why might a designer choose a synthetic material over a natural one?
options: Synthetic materials can offer specific properties like durability or low cost
Synthetic materials never have any useful properties
There is never a reason to choose synthetic materials
Natural materials cannot be used for anything

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparing Early Writing Systems
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: early civilizations developed different writing systems, such as hieroglyphics in Egypt, cuneiform in Mesopotamia, and early script in the Indus Valley, to record information.

quiz.q: Hieroglyphics was the early writing system used in ___.
Cuneiform was the early writing system used in ___.
Early writing systems were mainly developed to ___.
Why is the development of writing considered a major milestone in early civilizations?
options: Writing systems were identical across all civilizations
Writing had no effect on early societies
Early societies never needed to record information
It allowed information to be recorded and passed on more reliably

quiz.q: Comparing writing systems across civilizations shows that ___.
options: Writing systems have no historical significance
Only one civilization ever developed writing
All early writing systems were exactly the same
Different societies solved the need to record information in unique ways

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Book Reviews
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: a book review shares an opinion about a book, supported by reasons and details, and often includes a brief summary along with a recommendation.

quiz.q: A book review typically includes ___.
options: An opinion supported by reasons and a brief summary

quiz.q: Why should a book review include supporting reasons?
options: Reasons are not needed in a review
To help the reader understand why the reviewer feels that way
Supporting reasons make a review less convincing

quiz.q: A recommendation in a book review tells the reader ___.
options: Whether the reviewer thinks others should read the book

quiz.q: Which is an example of a detail that could support a book review?
options: Copying the entire book word for word
Describing an exciting scene from the book

quiz.q: A well-written book review helps other readers ___.
options: Understand only the price of books
Decide whether they might enjoy the book

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing and Ordering Decimals
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Number strand: to compare decimals, line up the decimal points and compare digits from left to right, starting with the whole number, then tenths, then hundredths.

quiz.q: Which decimal is greater: 0.45 or 0.5?
Which decimal is smallest: 0.3, 0.25, or 0.35?
To compare decimals, you should first compare the ___.
options: Number of digits after the decimal

quiz.q: Order these from least to greatest: 0.6, 0.06, 0.66.
Which decimal is equivalent to 0.5?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Structures: Types of Bridges
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: different bridge designs, such as beam, arch, and suspension bridges, use different structural principles to support loads and span distances.

quiz.q: A beam bridge is supported mainly by ___.
An arch bridge uses a curved shape to help ___.
A suspension bridge is supported mainly by ___.
Why might engineers choose different bridge designs for different locations?
options: Only one bridge design has ever been used
Different designs suit different distances, loads, and terrain
All bridges must use the exact same design everywhere
Bridge design has no connection to location

quiz.q: Which bridge type is well-suited for spanning very long distances, like across a wide river?
options: None of these can span long distances

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Trade and Economy
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: early civilizations such as Egypt, Mesopotamia, China, and India developed trade networks to exchange goods like grain, textiles, and metals with neighbouring regions.

quiz.q: Trade in early societies mainly involved exchanging ___.
options: Goods such as grain, textiles, and metals

quiz.q: Why did early civilizations develop trade networks?
options: Trade networks did not exist in early history
Early societies were entirely self-sufficient with no needs
To obtain resources and goods not available locally

quiz.q: A trade network connects ___.
options: A single isolated village with no outside contact
Nothing, since trade never happened historically
Different regions or civilizations exchanging goods

quiz.q: Which might an early civilization export if it had abundant farmland?
options: Items it does not produce at all

quiz.q: Studying early trade helps us understand ___.
options: That economies have no historical importance
How ancient economies and connections between regions developed
That trade began only in modern times
That ancient civilizations never interacted with each other

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Personification
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: personification gives human qualities or actions to non-human things or ideas, such as saying the wind whispered or the sun smiled down.

quiz.q: Personification is when a writer gives ___.
Which sentence is an example of personification?
options: The wind is made of moving air.
The wind whispered through the trees.
The wind comes from changes in air pressure.
The wind speed was 20 kilometres per hour.

quiz.q: Why do writers use personification?
options: Personification has no purpose in writing
To make descriptions more vivid and engaging
To confuse the reader on purpose
To remove all imagery from writing

quiz.q: Which phrase uses personification?
options: The flowers need water and sunlight.
The flowers danced in the breeze.
The flowers grow in the garden.
The flowers are red and yellow.

quiz.q: Personification is a type of ___.

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Symmetry
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Geometry strand: a shape has line symmetry if it can be folded along a line so both halves match exactly, and that fold line is called a line of symmetry.

quiz.q: A shape has line symmetry if ___.
options: It cannot be folded at all
It has more than four sides
It can be folded so both halves match exactly

quiz.q: The line along which a symmetrical shape can be folded is called a ___.
How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
Which letter of the alphabet has a vertical line of symmetry?
A circle has how many lines of symmetry?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Forces: Magnetism
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: magnetism is a force that attracts certain metals, such as iron, and every magnet has a north pole and a south pole.

quiz.q: Magnetism is a force that attracts ___.
Every magnet has ___.
options: A north pole and a south pole

quiz.q: What happens when two magnets’ like poles (north-north or south-south) are brought together?
What happens when two magnets’ opposite poles (north-south) are brought together?
Which material would a magnet most strongly attract?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Art and Architecture
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: early civilizations expressed their culture and beliefs through art and architecture, such as Egyptian pyramids, Greek temples, and Chinese pottery.

quiz.q: Egyptian pyramids were primarily built as ___.
Greek temples were often built to honour ___.
Art and architecture in early societies often reflected their ___.
Why do historians study ancient art and architecture?
options: It offers insight into how early societies lived and what they valued
Only written records matter to historians
Architecture from the past teaches us nothing
Ancient art has no historical value

quiz.q: Which is an example of early architecture still studied today?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Cause and Effect
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: a cause is why something happens, and an effect is what happens as a result, and identifying these relationships helps readers understand how events connect in a text.

quiz.q: A cause is best described as ___.
options: What happens as a result of something

quiz.q: An effect is best described as ___.
options: What happens as a result of a cause

quiz.q: In the sentence It rained, so the game was cancelled, what is the effect?
Why is understanding cause and effect useful when reading?
options: It has no impact on understanding a text
Cause and effect never appears in stories
It helps readers see how events in a text are connected
It replaces the need to read the whole text

quiz.q: Which phrase often signals a cause-and-effect relationship?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Perimeter and Area of Irregular Shapes
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Geometry strand: irregular shapes can be broken into smaller regular shapes, like rectangles, to calculate their total perimeter or area by adding the parts together.

quiz.q: To find the area of an irregular shape, you can ___.
options: Break it into smaller regular shapes and add their areas

quiz.q: An L-shaped figure can be split into how many rectangles to find its area?
Perimeter is the measurement of ___.
options: The distance around the outside of a shape

quiz.q: If two rectangles that make up an irregular shape have areas of 12 and 8 square units, the total area is ___.
Why is it useful to break an irregular shape into rectangles?
options: Rectangles are easier to measure and calculate area for

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Ecosystems: Producers, Consumers, and Decomposers
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Life Systems strand: in an ecosystem, producers (like plants) make their own food, consumers (like animals) eat other organisms, and decomposers (like fungi) break down dead material.

quiz.q: A producer in an ecosystem is an organism that ___.
A consumer in an ecosystem is an organism that ___.
options: Has no role in the ecosystem
Makes its own food using sunlight

quiz.q: A decomposer’s role in an ecosystem is to ___.
options: Break down dead plants and animals

quiz.q: Which of these is typically classified as a producer?
Why are decomposers important to an ecosystem?
options: They prevent all growth in an ecosystem
They recycle nutrients back into the soil for producers to use
They have no effect on the ecosystem

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Territories: Yukon, Northwest Territories, and Nunavut
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada’s three territories -- Yukon, the Northwest Territories, and Nunavut -- make up the northern part of the country and have unique geography, climate, and Indigenous communities.

quiz.q: How many territories does Canada have?
Which of these is one of Canada’s three territories?
Canada’s territories are located mainly in the ___ part of the country.
The climate in Canada’s territories is generally ___.
Why is it important to learn about Canada’s territories?
options: They have no distinct geography or culture
They have no significance to Canada
They are a significant part of Canada’s geography, culture, and Indigenous communities
They are not actually part of Canada

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Multiple-Meaning Words
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Language strand: multiple-meaning words are spelled the same but can have different meanings depending on how they are used in a sentence, such as bat, bank, or watch.

quiz.q: The word bat can mean ___.
options: A flying animal or sports equipment, depending on context

quiz.q: In the sentence I watched the clock on my watch, the two uses of watch mean ___.
options: The exact same thing both times
Both refer to a type of bird
Different things: looked at, and a wristwatch

quiz.q: A multiple-meaning word is one that ___.
options: Can have different meanings depending on context
Cannot be used in a sentence
Is always spelled differently for each meaning

quiz.q: The word bank can refer to ___.
options: A financial institution or the side of a river, depending on context

quiz.q: How can a reader figure out which meaning of a word is intended?
options: By guessing randomly with no strategy
By ignoring the rest of the sentence
By using context clues from the surrounding sentence

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: 24-Hour Clock (Military Time)
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Measurement strand: the 24-hour clock counts hours from 00:00 (midnight) to 23:59, avoiding the need for AM and PM, and is commonly used in schedules like transit and travel.

quiz.q: What time is 14:00 in standard 12-hour time?
On the 24-hour clock, midnight is written as ___.
What is 6:00 PM written in 24-hour time?
The 24-hour clock is often used for ___.
options: Schedules such as transit and travel
