quiz.q: What is 09:00 in 24-hour time equivalent to in standard time?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Renewable and Non-Renewable Energy Sources
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Matter and Energy strand: renewable energy sources, like solar and wind, can be replenished naturally, while non-renewable sources, like coal and oil, take millions of years to form and can run out.

quiz.q: A renewable energy source is one that ___.
options: Can be replenished naturally over a short time
Takes millions of years to form
Can never be used again once used once

quiz.q: Which is an example of a renewable energy source?
A non-renewable energy source is one that ___.
options: Takes an extremely long time to form and can run out

quiz.q: Which is an example of a non-renewable energy source?
Why is there growing interest in using more renewable energy sources?
options: Renewable sources are always worse for the environment
They can be replenished naturally and often produce less pollution
There is no benefit to renewable energy

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Map Skills: Scale and Symbols
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: maps use a scale to show real-world distances in a smaller form, and symbols in a legend to represent features like cities, roads, or rivers.

quiz.q: A map scale helps show ___.
options: Real-world distances in a smaller form

quiz.q: Symbols on a map are explained in a ___.
If a map scale shows 1 cm equals 10 km, a 3 cm distance on the map represents ___.
Why do maps use symbols instead of writing out every detail?
options: Symbols have no purpose on maps
Symbols make maps impossible to understand
Symbols make maps easier to read quickly
Maps are not allowed to use symbols

quiz.q: Which might a map symbol commonly represent?

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Dialogue Writing
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: dialogue writing brings characters to life through their spoken words, using quotation marks and a new paragraph each time the speaker changes.

quiz.q: Dialogue in a story refers to ___.
When writing dialogue, a new paragraph is usually started ___.
options: Only at the very end of the story
Never, all dialogue stays in one paragraph

quiz.q: Why is dialogue useful in a story?
options: It reveals character personality and moves the plot forward
Dialogue replaces the need for a plot
Dialogue has no purpose in writing
Dialogue slows down every story unnecessarily

quiz.q: Which punctuation is typically used to show a character’s exact spoken words?
Good dialogue should sound ___.
options: Identical for every character in the story
Completely random and unrelated to the character
Natural, like how the character would actually speak

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Pictographs
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Data Management strand: a pictograph uses pictures or symbols to represent data, with a key showing how many items each symbol stands for.

quiz.q: A pictograph uses ___ to represent data.
The key in a pictograph tells you ___.
options: How many items each symbol represents
The names of the people who made the graph

quiz.q: If a pictograph key shows 1 symbol equals 5 students, and there are 4 symbols for Grade 4, how many Grade 4 students are there?
Pictographs are especially useful for ___.
options: Replacing the need for any data at all
Making data visually easy to compare at a glance
Making data more difficult to understand

quiz.q: Half of a symbol in a pictograph usually represents ___.
options: The exact same as a full symbol
Half of the value the full symbol represents

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Simple Machines: Wheel and Axle, and Screw
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand: a wheel and axle reduces the effort needed to move objects by rotating together, while a screw is a spiral inclined plane that converts turning motion into straight-line motion.

quiz.q: A wheel and axle work together by ___.
options: Absorbing all force with no motion
Rotating together to reduce the effort needed to move objects

quiz.q: A screw can be thought of as a type of ___ wrapped around a cylinder.
Which everyday object uses a wheel and axle?
A screw converts ___ motion into straight-line motion.
Why is a wheel and axle considered a simple machine?
options: It always makes tasks more difficult
It makes moving or lifting objects easier by reducing the effort required

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Climate Zones of Canada
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: Canada spans several climate zones, from the cold Arctic in the north to milder, temperate zones in the south, influencing where people live and how they adapt.

quiz.q: Canada’s northern regions generally have a ___ climate.
Southern Canada generally has a ___ climate compared to the north.
Climate zones influence ___.
options: Where people settle and how they adapt their lifestyles

quiz.q: Why might fewer people live in Canada’s far northern climate zones?
options: The north is actually the warmest region in Canada
Climate has no effect on where people choose to live
The north has no unique climate challenges
The harsh cold climate makes farming and travel more challenging

quiz.q: Studying climate zones helps students understand ___.
options: That all of Canada has the exact same weather year-round
How geography and weather patterns vary across a large country like Canada
That climate has no connection to geography
That Canada has only one climate zone

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Media Literacy: Recognizing Bias in News
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Media Literacy strand: bias in news occurs when information is presented in a way that favours one side or opinion, and recognizing it helps readers evaluate sources critically.

quiz.q: Bias in news means the information is presented ___.
options: In a way that favours one side or opinion
Without any point of view at all
In a completely neutral, balanced way

quiz.q: Why is it important for readers to recognize bias?
options: Bias has no effect on how information is understood
To think critically and evaluate information more carefully
All news is always completely unbiased

quiz.q: Which might be a sign of bias in a news story?
options: Only presenting one side of an issue
Presenting balanced viewpoints from multiple sources

quiz.q: Comparing multiple news sources on the same topic can help readers ___.
options: Become more confused with no benefit
Get a more complete, balanced understanding
Guarantee that one source is entirely correct

quiz.q: A media-literate reader asks questions like ___.
options: What did I have for breakfast?
Who created this message, and why?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Predicting Outcomes with Probability
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Data Management strand: probability can be used to predict likely outcomes, such as predicting that rolling a die is more likely to land on a number 1 through 6 than any other value.

quiz.q: If a bag has 3 red marbles and 1 blue marble, which colour is more likely to be picked?
Rolling a standard six-sided die, what is the chance of rolling a 7?
Predicting outcomes with probability means ___.
options: Guessing with no reasoning at all
Using known information to estimate what is likely to happen

quiz.q: If a spinner has 4 equal sections numbered 1 to 4, what is the chance of landing on 2?
An outcome that is certain to happen has a probability of ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Recycling and Waste Reduction
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Earth and Space Systems strand: recycling and waste reduction help limit the amount of garbage sent to landfills by reusing materials and reducing unnecessary consumption.

quiz.q: Recycling helps reduce the amount of waste sent to ___.
Which is an example of waste reduction?
options: Ignoring how much waste is produced
Reusing containers instead of throwing them away

quiz.q: Materials like paper, glass, and some plastics can often be ___.
Why is reducing waste important for the environment?
options: Waste has no impact on the environment
It helps conserve resources and limit pollution from landfills

quiz.q: Which of the three Rs comes first in reducing environmental impact: reduce, reuse, or recycle?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canadian Identity: Symbols and Traditions
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies Heritage and Identity strand: Canadian identity is expressed through symbols and traditions such as the maple leaf, the national anthem, and celebrations that reflect the country’s diverse heritage.

quiz.q: Which is a well-known symbol of Canada?
National symbols and traditions help express ___.
options: A country’s shared identity and values

quiz.q: Canada’s national anthem is an example of a ___.
Why does Canada celebrate diverse cultural traditions?
options: Cultural diversity has no connection to Canadian identity
Celebrations are not part of Canadian identity
Canada is home to people from many different cultural backgrounds

quiz.q: Learning about Canadian symbols and traditions helps students understand ___.
options: That traditions are unimportant to a country
What makes up a shared sense of Canadian identity
That Canada has no shared identity

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Possessive Nouns and Apostrophes
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Writing strand: possessive nouns show ownership using an apostrophe, such as adding ’s for singular nouns (the dog’s bone) or just an apostrophe for plural nouns ending in s (the dogs’ bones).

quiz.q: Which shows correct singular possession?
How do you typically form the possessive of a plural noun ending in s?
options: Add just an apostrophe after the s

quiz.q: Which sentence correctly shows plural possession?
options: The students book’s were on the desk.
The students’ books were on the desk.
The students books were on the desk.
The student’s’ books were on the desk.

quiz.q: An apostrophe in a possessive noun shows ___.
options: That a letter has been removed only

quiz.q: Which is the correct possessive form for a single cat named Max?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Growing and Shrinking Patterns
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Algebra strand: a growing pattern increases by a consistent rule each step, while a shrinking pattern decreases by a consistent rule each step.

quiz.q: In the pattern 3, 6, 9, 12, what is the next number?
In the pattern 50, 45, 40, 35, what is the next number?
A growing pattern is one where the values ___.
A shrinking pattern is one where the values ___.
What is the rule for the pattern 2, 4, 8, 16?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Scientific Inquiry: Designing a Fair Test
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Inquiry strand: a fair test changes only one variable at a time while keeping all other conditions the same, so results can be reliably compared and attributed to that one change.

quiz.q: In a fair test, how many variables should typically be changed at a time?
Why is it important to keep other conditions the same in a fair test?
options: Keeping conditions the same makes the test less accurate
So any difference in results can be attributed to the one variable being tested
Fair tests do not need consistent conditions
It is not important at all

quiz.q: If testing how sunlight affects plant growth, what should stay the same between test plants?
options: The plant species should be different each time
Nothing needs to stay the same

quiz.q: A variable in an experiment is ___.
options: A factor that can change and affect the results

quiz.q: Why do scientists rely on fair tests?
options: To draw reliable, accurate conclusions from their results
Fair tests are only used outside of science
Fair testing is unnecessary in science
Fair tests make conclusions less reliable

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Local Government: Municipal Roles
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies People and Environments strand: municipal (local) governments are responsible for community services such as roads, parks, garbage collection, and local bylaws, led by a mayor and council.

quiz.q: Which level of government is typically responsible for garbage collection?
A municipal government is usually led by a ___.
Which is an example of a municipal responsibility?
A local bylaw is ___.
options: A rule with no legal effect
A national law that applies to every country
The same as a federal law
A rule created by the municipal government for the local community

quiz.q: Why is municipal government important to daily life?
options: It has no connection to daily life
Municipal government does not exist in Canada
It only matters once every four years
It manages many of the services communities rely on every day

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Reflecting and Responding to Texts
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Reading strand: reflecting on a text means thinking about how it connects to your own experiences, other texts, or the world, and forming a thoughtful personal response.

quiz.q: Reflecting on a text means ___.
options: Ignoring your own thoughts about the text
Only memorizing the text word for word
Thinking about how the text connects to your experiences or the world

quiz.q: A personal response to a text might include ___.
options: Only copying sentences from the text
No opinions or thoughts at all
Your own thoughts, feelings, or connections to the text

quiz.q: Connecting a text to your own experience is an example of a ___ connection.
Why is reflecting on a text a valuable reading habit?
options: It deepens understanding and makes reading more meaningful
Reflection has no benefit for readers
It replaces the need to read carefully

quiz.q: Which is an example of a text-to-text connection?
options: Comparing a story to another book you have read
Only focusing on the cover of the book
Comparing a story to a math worksheet only

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Budgeting Basics
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Financial Literacy strand: a budget is a plan for how to use money, balancing income (money coming in) with expenses (money going out), and setting aside savings when possible.

quiz.q: A budget is best described as ___.
options: A list of things you already own
A plan for how to use money
A type of bank account only
A rule that forbids spending money

quiz.q: Income refers to ___.
An expense refers to ___.
options: Money going out for something purchased

quiz.q: If Malik earns $20 in allowance and spends $12, how much is left to save?
Why is it useful to set aside savings in a budget?
options: A budget should never include savings
To have money available for future needs or goals
Saving money is the same as spending it

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Structures, Forces, and Simple Machines
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Science Structures and Mechanisms strand review: this lesson revisits structures, forces like magnetism and friction, and simple machines like levers, inclined planes, wheels and axles, and screws, from across the year.

quiz.q: Which is an example of a simple machine studied this year?
A structure’s ability to support weight without collapsing depends on its ___.
options: Design and how it distributes load

quiz.q: Magnetism is an example of a ___ studied in this unit.
Which simple machine converts turning motion into straight-line motion?
Why is it useful to review structures, forces, and simple machines together?
options: These topics have no connection to each other
Review is never useful in science
Each topic must be learned in complete isolation
They are closely connected ideas that build a fuller understanding of mechanisms

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Culminating Task: Comparing an Early Society to Canada Today
subjects.summary: Ontario Grade 4 Social Studies culminating task: students compare daily life, government, and technology in an early society, such as Ancient Egypt or Mesopotamia, with life in Canada today.

quiz.q: Comparing an early society to Canada today can include looking at ___.
Which is a valid comparison between Ancient Egypt and Canada today?
options: Canada today has pyramids as its main structures
Both have systems of government, though very different in structure
Neither society ever had any form of government

quiz.q: Why is comparing past and present societies a valuable learning task?
options: This kind of comparison is impossible to make
It helps students understand how societies change and what stays similar over time
Comparing societies has no educational value
Past societies have no connection to life today

quiz.q: A culminating task is typically used to ___.
options: Bring together and apply learning from throughout a unit or year
Introduce a brand new unit with no prior learning
Replace the need for any learning at all

quiz.q: Which skill is most useful when completing a comparison task like this?
options: Identifying similarities and differences with supporting evidence
Ignoring one of the two things being compared

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Making Connections (Text-to-Self, Text-to-Text, Text-to-World)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: strong readers make connections between a text and their own life, other texts they have read, and events happening in the wider world.

quiz.q: A text-to-self connection is when a reader links a text to ___.
options: The number of chapters in the book

quiz.q: A text-to-text connection compares a story to ___.
options: Only the cover illustration of the book
Another text the reader has read before
Nothing at all, since texts cannot be compared
A concept unrelated to reading comprehension

quiz.q: A text-to-world connection links a story to ___.
options: Only the page count of the text
Events or issues happening in the wider world
A detail unrelated to the story itself
Nothing, since stories have no connection to the world

quiz.q: Why do readers make connections while reading?
options: Connections only distract readers from the story
It helps deepen understanding and makes the text more meaningful
Making connections never helps a reader understand a text
This strategy has no connection to reading comprehension

quiz.q: If a story about moving to a new school reminds you of your own first day at school, what kind of connection is this?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplying Fractions by Whole Numbers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: multiplying a fraction by a whole number means adding that fraction the given number of times, such as 3 times 1/4 equalling 3/4.

quiz.q: Multiplying a fraction by a whole number is the same as ___.
options: Dividing the fraction into smaller unrelated parts
Subtracting the whole number from the fraction
Adding that fraction the given number of times

quiz.q: What is 3 × 1/4?
options: A value unrelated to the multiplication

quiz.q: What is 2 × 2/5?
options: A value unrelated to the multiplication

quiz.q: When multiplying a fraction by a whole number, which part of the fraction changes?
options: The numerator, while the denominator stays the same
The denominator, while the numerator stays the same

quiz.q: What is 4 × 1/3?
options: A value unrelated to the multiplication

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Ecosystems: Interdependence of Living Things
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: living things in an ecosystem depend on each other and their environment in a web of interdependent relationships.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Interdependence in an ecosystem means living things ___.
options: Live completely apart from their environment
Rely on each other and their environment to survive
Never interact with each other in any way

quiz.q: Which is an example of interdependence in an ecosystem?
options: A concept unrelated to living things
A rock resting on the ground with no living connections
Two organisms that never interact in any way
Bees pollinating flowers while flowers provide bees with nectar

quiz.q: What might happen if one species disappeared from an ecosystem?
options: Other species that depended on it could also be affected
Nothing in the ecosystem would ever change
Every other species would automatically disappear too

quiz.q: Why do scientists study interdependence in ecosystems?
options: Ecosystems have no relationships worth studying
Studying interdependence provides no useful scientific information
This concept has no connection to science
It helps them understand how changes affect the whole community of living things

quiz.q: Which best describes a healthy, balanced ecosystem?
options: One where every species lives in complete isolation
One where living things and their environment support each other
One where no living things ever interact

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Mapping Skills: Latitude and Longitude
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: latitude and longitude are imaginary lines that form a grid on maps and globes, helping people locate exact places on Earth.

quiz.q: Latitude and longitude lines help people ___.
options: Measure the temperature of a location
Determine the population of a city

quiz.q: Lines of latitude run ___.
options: North to south, connecting the poles
East to west, parallel to the equator

quiz.q: Lines of longitude run ___.
options: East to west, parallel to the equator
North to south, connecting the poles

quiz.q: Why is a grid of latitude and longitude lines useful on maps?
options: It only shows the colours of different countries
It allows any location on Earth to be described with precise coordinates
It has no practical use for finding locations

quiz.q: The equator is a line of ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Subject-Verb Agreement
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: subject-verb agreement means matching a singular subject with a singular verb and a plural subject with a plural verb.

quiz.q: Subject-verb agreement means the subject and verb must ___.
options: Be separated by a comma at all times
Always be written in past tense

quiz.q: Which sentence shows correct subject-verb agreement?
options: The dogs runs in the park.
The dog run in the park.
The dog runs in the park.
A sentence unrelated to subject-verb agreement

quiz.q: Which sentence shows correct subject-verb agreement?
options: A sentence unrelated to subject-verb agreement

quiz.q: A singular subject like “she” should be paired with a verb like ___.
options: A verb form unrelated to agreement
Walking, with no other words needed

quiz.q: Why is subject-verb agreement important in writing?
options: It has no effect on how a sentence reads
A concept unrelated to clear writing
It only matters in spoken language, never in writing
It makes sentences grammatically correct and easier to understand

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Introduction to Prime and Composite Numbers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a prime number has exactly two factors (1 and itself), while a composite number has more than two factors.

quiz.q: A prime number has exactly ___ factors.
options: A number of factors unrelated to primes

quiz.q: Which of these is a prime number?
Which of these is a composite number?
Why is the number 1 neither prime nor composite?
options: 1 is always considered a prime number
It has only one factor, itself, not two or more
It has an unlimited number of factors

quiz.q: Which of these numbers is prime?

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Rock and Mineral Classification: Igneous, Sedimentary, Metamorphic
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: rocks are classified into three types based on how they form -- igneous (from cooled magma), sedimentary (from compressed layers), and metamorphic (changed by heat and pressure).
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Igneous rock forms when ___.
options: A process unrelated to rock formation
Existing rock is changed by heat and pressure
Layers of sediment are compressed over time

quiz.q: Sedimentary rock forms when ___.
options: Existing rock is changed by heat and pressure
A process unrelated to rock formation
Layers of sediment are compressed over time

quiz.q: Metamorphic rock forms when ___.
options: A process unrelated to rock formation
Existing rock is changed by heat and pressure
Layers of sediment are compressed over time

quiz.q: Which is an example of a way scientists classify rocks?
options: By how the rock was formed
By where the rock is displayed in a museum
A method unrelated to rock classification
By the colour of the rock alone, with no other criteria

quiz.q: Why do geologists classify rocks into these three types?
options: Rocks are never classified into different types
It helps them understand how each rock formed and what it can reveal about Earth’s history
Classifying rocks provides no useful scientific information

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparing Government Systems: Federal, Provincial, and Municipal
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: Canada has three levels of government -- federal, provincial, and municipal -- each responsible for different services and decisions.

quiz.q: The federal government is responsible for issues that affect ___.
The provincial government is responsible for issues such as ___.
options: Decisions made only by other countries
Education and healthcare within the province

quiz.q: The municipal government is responsible for issues such as ___.
options: Local roads, garbage collection, and community parks

quiz.q: Why does Canada have three levels of government?
options: A reason unrelated to government structure
Only one level of government is actually needed
Having multiple levels of government serves no useful purpose
Different levels can focus on issues at the national, provincial, and local scale

quiz.q: Which level of government would most likely decide on a new local playground?
options: A level unrelated to local decisions

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Root Words
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a root word is the base part of a word that carries its core meaning, and many other words can be built from it by adding prefixes or suffixes.

quiz.q: A root word is ___.
options: A word with no meaning at all
The base part of a word that carries its core meaning
Always the longest word in a sentence

quiz.q: What is the root word in “unhappiness”?
options: A word unrelated to the root

quiz.q: What is the root word in “replayed”?
options: A word unrelated to the root

quiz.q: Knowing a root word can help a reader ___.
options: Never understand the meaning of any word
Figure out the meaning of related, unfamiliar words

quiz.q: What is the root word in “disagreement”?
options: A word unrelated to the root

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Measuring and Classifying Angles with a Protractor
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a protractor is a tool used to measure the size of an angle in degrees, and angles can be classified as acute, right, obtuse, or straight.

quiz.q: A protractor is used to ___.
options: Measure the weight of an object
Measure the length of a line
Measure the size of an angle in degrees

quiz.q: An angle less than 90 degrees is called ___.
An angle greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees is called ___.
An angle that measures exactly 90 degrees is called ___.
An angle that measures exactly 180 degrees is called ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Waves and Vibrations: How Sound is Produced
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: sound is produced when an object vibrates, creating waves of energy that travel through air or another medium to our ears.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Sound is produced when an object ___.
Sound waves travel through ___.
options: A vacuum with no matter at all
Air and other materials, such as water

quiz.q: What happens to the vibration of a guitar string when it is plucked?
options: It vibrates back and forth, producing sound waves
A result unrelated to sound production
It changes into a different material

quiz.q: Why can we not hear sound in outer space?
options: A reason unrelated to how sound travels
Sound only exists on Earth for no scientific reason
There is no air or matter for sound waves to travel through
Sound waves travel faster in space, making them silent

quiz.q: Which best describes a sound wave?
options: A form of energy that travels through vibrations
A form of light that has no connection to vibration
A solid object that can be touched

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Industries and Economic Regions
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: different regions of Canada rely on different industries, such as fishing on the coasts, farming on the Prairies, and manufacturing in central Canada.

quiz.q: An industry is ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the economy
Only a building where people live
A type of economic activity that produces goods or services

quiz.q: Which industry is closely associated with the Prairie provinces?
options: An industry unrelated to the Prairies

quiz.q: Which industry is closely associated with Canada’s coastal regions?
options: An industry unrelated to coastal regions

quiz.q: Why do different regions of Canada rely on different industries?
options: Industries are assigned randomly to each region
Every region of Canada has identical industries
Each region’s geography and resources shape which industries can thrive there

quiz.q: Which industry is closely associated with central Canada?
options: An industry unrelated to central Canada

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Compare and Contrast Paragraphs
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a compare and contrast paragraph explains how two subjects are similar and how they are different, often using words like “however” and “similarly.”

quiz.q: A compare and contrast paragraph explains how two subjects are ___.
Which word signals a comparison (similarity) between two ideas?
Which word signals a contrast (difference) between two ideas?
Why might a writer compare and contrast two animals in an essay?
options: To help readers understand how the animals are alike and different
Comparing animals serves no purpose in writing
To make the essay longer with no added meaning

quiz.q: What should a compare and contrast paragraph include?
options: Details about only one of the two subjects
No details at all, only opinions
Specific details about both similarities and differences
A concept unrelated to this type of writing

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Volume of Rectangular Prisms
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: the volume of a rectangular prism is found by multiplying its length, width, and height, and is measured in cubic units.

quiz.q: The volume of a rectangular prism is found by multiplying ___.
options: Only the height of the shape

quiz.q: What is the volume of a rectangular prism with length 4, width 3, and height 2?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Volume is measured in ___.
What is the volume of a rectangular prism with length 5, width 2, and height 3?
options: A value unrelated to the calculation

quiz.q: Why is volume measured in cubic units rather than square units?
options: Square units are always used for volume instead
Because volume describes a three-dimensional space, not a flat area
Cubic units have no real meaning in math

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Simple Circuits: Series vs Parallel
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: in a series circuit, components are connected in a single loop, while in a parallel circuit, components are connected along multiple paths.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: In a series circuit, components are connected ___.
options: With no connection between them at all
Along a single loop, one after another

quiz.q: In a parallel circuit, components are connected ___.
options: With no connection between them at all

quiz.q: What happens to the other bulbs in a series circuit if one bulb burns out?
options: They all turn off, since the loop is broken
They stay lit with no change at all

quiz.q: What happens to the other bulbs in a parallel circuit if one bulb burns out?
options: The others can stay lit, since each has its own path

quiz.q: Why might parallel circuits be used for household wiring?
options: Parallel circuits have no practical advantage
Series circuits are always used in houses instead
So that one broken device does not turn off all the others

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Indigenous Governance and Traditional Knowledge
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: many Indigenous peoples in Canada have long-standing governance systems and traditional knowledge that guide decision-making and care for the land.

quiz.q: Traditional knowledge refers to ___.
options: Wisdom and practices passed down through generations within a community
Knowledge that has no connection to any community
Information found only in modern textbooks
A concept unrelated to Indigenous peoples

quiz.q: Indigenous governance systems often emphasize ___.
options: Community decision-making and care for the land
Ignoring the needs of the land and environment
Decisions made entirely without community input

quiz.q: Why is it important to learn about Indigenous governance systems?
options: It builds a fuller understanding of the diverse ways communities have organized themselves
A reason unrelated to social studies
Only one type of governance system has ever existed
Indigenous governance has no relevance to Canadian history

quiz.q: Traditional knowledge is often passed down through ___.
options: Sources with no connection to community elders
Storytelling, teachings from elders, and lived experience
A method unrelated to traditional knowledge

quiz.q: Why might traditional knowledge be valuable for understanding the environment?
options: It is based on guesses with no real observation involved
Traditional knowledge has no connection to the environment
A reason unrelated to Indigenous knowledge
It reflects generations of careful observation and relationship with the land

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Distinguishing Fact from Opinion
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a fact is a statement that can be proven true, while an opinion is a personal belief or judgment that cannot be proven.

quiz.q: A fact is a statement that ___.
options: Can never be proven either way

quiz.q: An opinion is a statement that ___.
options: Reflects a personal belief or judgment
Can always be proven true with evidence
Is the same for every single reader

quiz.q: Which of these is a fact?
options: Chocolate ice cream tastes better than vanilla.
A statement unrelated to facts or opinions
Winter is the best season of the year.

quiz.q: Which of these is an opinion?
options: A statement unrelated to facts or opinions

quiz.q: Why is it important for readers to distinguish fact from opinion?
options: A concept unrelated to reading comprehension
Facts and opinions are always exactly the same thing
This skill has no use when reading any text
It helps them evaluate information critically and avoid being misled

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Temperature and Thermometers (Celsius)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: temperature is measured in degrees Celsius in Canada, using a thermometer, with 0°C as the freezing point and 100°C as the boiling point of water.

quiz.q: In Celsius, water freezes at ___.
In Celsius, water boils at ___.
A thermometer is used to measure ___.
Which temperature would most likely describe a warm summer day in Ontario?
options: A temperature unrelated to a warm day

quiz.q: Which temperature would most likely describe a cold winter day in Ontario?
options: A temperature unrelated to a cold day

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Forces: Balanced and Unbalanced Forces
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: balanced forces on an object result in no change in motion, while unbalanced forces cause an object to speed up, slow down, or change direction.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Balanced forces on an object result in ___.
options: No change in the object’s motion

quiz.q: Unbalanced forces on an object can cause it to ___.
options: Remain in exactly the same state forever
Speed up, slow down, or change direction

quiz.q: If two people push equally hard on opposite sides of a box and it does not move, the forces are ___.
If a soccer ball is kicked and starts rolling, what kind of force acted on it?
Why is it useful to understand balanced and unbalanced forces?
options: It helps explain why objects stay still or change their motion
Forces never affect the motion of objects
This concept has no connection to how objects move

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Settlement Patterns: Why Communities Grow Where They Do
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: communities often form and grow near resources such as fresh water, fertile land, and transportation routes.

quiz.q: Communities often settle near fresh water because it provides ___.
options: Drinking water, transportation, and support for farming

quiz.q: Fertile land is valuable to a community because it supports ___.
options: A concept unrelated to settlement patterns

quiz.q: Why might a community grow near a major transportation route, such as a river or railway?
options: Transportation routes have no effect on where communities grow
Communities never consider transportation when settling
It makes trade and travel to other places easier
A reason unrelated to settlement patterns

quiz.q: Which of these would most likely attract early settlers to an area?
options: Access to fresh water and fertile land
The colour of the soil alone
An area with no resources of any kind

quiz.q: Why do geographers study settlement patterns?
options: Communities are located in completely random places
It helps explain why communities are located where they are
Settlement patterns provide no useful information

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Hyperbole
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: hyperbole is an extreme exaggeration used for effect, such as saying “I have a million things to do.”

quiz.q: Hyperbole is a figure of speech that uses ___.
options: A concept unrelated to figurative language

quiz.q: Which sentence contains a hyperbole?
options: I had a sandwich for lunch today.
I am so hungry I could eat a horse.
I ate a small snack this afternoon.

quiz.q: Why do writers use hyperbole?
options: To describe something in a completely literal way
Hyperbole is never used for any purpose in writing
A reason unrelated to figurative language
To emphasize a feeling or idea in a dramatic, memorable way

quiz.q: Which sentence contains a hyperbole?
options: This backpack weighs a million pounds!
This backpack is a bit heavy today.

quiz.q: Is hyperbole meant to be taken literally?
options: Hyperbole has no real meaning at all
No, it is an exaggeration for effect
Yes, hyperbole is always literally true

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Ratios and Simple Proportional Reasoning (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a ratio compares two quantities, such as 2 red marbles for every 3 blue marbles, and can be used to solve simple proportional problems.

quiz.q: A ratio is used to ___.
options: Measure the length of an object

quiz.q: If a recipe uses a ratio of 2 cups of flour for every 1 cup of sugar, how much flour is needed for 2 cups of sugar?
options: An amount unrelated to the ratio

quiz.q: A ratio of 3:1 means that for every 3 of one item, there is/are ___ of the other.
options: A number unrelated to the ratio

quiz.q: If a class has a ratio of 2 boys for every 3 girls, and there are 6 boys, how many girls are there?
options: A number unrelated to the ratio

quiz.q: Why are ratios useful in everyday life?
options: Ratios have no practical use in daily life
They help compare quantities and scale recipes, maps, and mixtures accurately
Ratios can only be used in advanced mathematics

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: States of Matter and the Particle Model
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: matter exists in solid, liquid, and gas states, and the particle model explains that particles in a solid are tightly packed, in a liquid can move past each other, and in a gas move freely.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: In a solid, particles are ___.
options: Spread far apart and moving freely
Tightly packed and vibrate in place

quiz.q: In a liquid, particles ___.
options: Spread out infinitely in all directions
Can move and slide past one another

quiz.q: In a gas, particles ___.
options: Move freely and spread far apart

quiz.q: The particle model helps explain ___.
options: Only the colour of different materials
Why matter behaves differently as a solid, liquid, or gas

quiz.q: Which state of matter has particles that are the most tightly packed?
options: A state unrelated to particle spacing

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Fur Trade in Canadian History
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: the fur trade was a major economic activity in early Canadian history, involving Indigenous peoples and European traders exchanging goods such as furs, tools, and cloth.

quiz.q: The fur trade primarily involved the exchange of ___.
options: Furs for European tools, cloth, and other goods
A concept unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: Which group played a central role in the fur trade as both trappers and traders?
options: Only settlers who arrived after the fur trade ended
A group unrelated to the fur trade
People with no connection to the fur trade at all

quiz.q: Why was the fur trade economically important in early Canada?
options: A reason unrelated to the fur trade
It only involved a handful of people with no wider impact
It was a major source of income and connected different regions through trade routes
The fur trade had no economic importance in Canadian history

quiz.q: Which animal’s fur was especially valuable during the fur trade?
options: An animal unrelated to the fur trade

quiz.q: How did the fur trade affect relationships between Indigenous peoples and European traders?
options: It created important economic and social relationships, though they were not always equal
Indigenous peoples and European traders never interacted
A reason unrelated to the fur trade
It had no effect on relationships between the two groups

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Spelling: Compound Words
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a compound word is formed by joining two smaller words together, such as “sun” and “flower” combining to make “sunflower.”

quiz.q: A compound word is formed by ___.
options: Adding a prefix only, with no second word
Removing letters from a single word

quiz.q: Which of these is a compound word?
options: A word unrelated to compound words

quiz.q: What two smaller words make up “rainbow”?
Which of these is a compound word?
options: A word unrelated to compound words

quiz.q: Why is it useful to recognize compound words when spelling?
options: A concept unrelated to spelling strategies
Compound words cannot be broken into smaller parts
Recognizing compound words never helps with spelling
It helps break a longer word into two familiar, smaller words

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Translations, Reflections, and Rotations
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a translation slides a shape, a reflection flips a shape like a mirror image, and a rotation turns a shape around a fixed point.

quiz.q: A translation moves a shape by ___.
options: Turning it around a fixed point
Flipping it like a mirror image
Sliding it without flipping or turning it

quiz.q: A reflection creates a shape that is ___.
options: An exact copy with no changes at all
A mirror image of the original shape
Always larger than the original shape

quiz.q: A rotation moves a shape by ___.
options: Turning it around a fixed point
Flipping it like a mirror image
Sliding it in a straight line

quiz.q: If you slide a triangle three squares to the right without flipping or turning it, this is an example of a ___.
If you flip a shape over a line so it looks like a mirror image, this is an example of a ___.

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Water Conservation and the Water Cycle in Communities
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: communities rely on the water cycle for fresh water, and conserving water helps protect this limited resource for future use.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Water conservation means ___.
options: Removing water from the environment permanently
Using water carefully to avoid wasting it
Using as much water as possible at all times

quiz.q: Which is an example of water conservation at home?
options: A concept unrelated to conserving water
Watering the lawn during a rainstorm
Turning off the tap while brushing your teeth
Leaving the tap running for no reason

quiz.q: Why is it important for communities to conserve fresh water?
options: Fresh water is a limited resource that must be shared and protected
A reason unrelated to water conservation
Fresh water is an unlimited resource with no need for conservation
Communities never need to think about their water use

quiz.q: How does the water cycle provide fresh water to communities?
options: A reason unrelated to the water cycle
Precipitation refills rivers, lakes, and groundwater that communities depend on
Communities create their own water with no natural cycle involved
The water cycle has no connection to community water supplies

quiz.q: Which of these actions helps conserve water in a community?
options: Fixing leaky pipes and faucets promptly
Wasting water intentionally to test the pipes

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Relationship with the Commonwealth
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: Canada is a member of the Commonwealth, a voluntary association of countries, most of which were once part of the British Empire, that cooperate on shared goals.

quiz.q: The Commonwealth is a voluntary association of countries that ___.
options: Are required to follow identical laws with no independence
Cooperate on shared goals, such as democracy and development
Have no historical connection to one another
A concept unrelated to international relationships

quiz.q: Many Commonwealth countries share a historical connection to ___.
options: A country unrelated to the Commonwealth

quiz.q: Is Canada required to follow the laws of other Commonwealth countries?
options: Yes, Canada must follow every Commonwealth country’s laws
No, Canada remains an independent, self-governing country
A concept unrelated to Canada’s independence
Canada has no independent government of its own

quiz.q: Why might countries choose to remain part of the Commonwealth?
options: Countries are forced to join with no choice involved
To cooperate on shared goals like trade, education, and development
A reason unrelated to international cooperation
Membership provides no benefits of any kind

quiz.q: Which of these is true about the Commonwealth?
options: It includes only countries that have never had any historical connection
A statement unrelated to the Commonwealth
It includes many countries that were once part of the British Empire
It has existed for less than one year

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Personal Narratives and Journals
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a personal narrative tells a true story from the writer’s own life, often written in first person and organized in the order events happened.

quiz.q: A personal narrative tells a story that is ___.
options: True and based on the writer’s own life
Written about someone the writer has never met

quiz.q: A personal narrative is usually written in ___.
options: A point of view unrelated to personal narratives

quiz.q: Personal narratives are often organized ___.
options: A concept unrelated to organizing a narrative
In the order the events happened
With no order or structure at all

quiz.q: Why might someone keep a journal?
options: To write only about topics unrelated to their own life
To record personal thoughts, feelings, and experiences over time
Journals serve no purpose for writers

quiz.q: Which is an example of a topic for a personal narrative?
options: A memorable trip you took with your family
A fictional story about dragons with no basis in real life
A set of instructions for building a model
A concept unrelated to personal narratives

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Circle Graphs (Pie Charts)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a circle graph, or pie chart, shows data as slices of a circle, where each slice represents a proportion of the whole.

quiz.q: A circle graph shows data as ___.
options: Slices of a circle representing proportions of a whole
A concept unrelated to circle graphs
A list of numbers with no visual representation

quiz.q: In a circle graph, a larger slice represents ___.
options: A concept unrelated to circle graphs
A smaller proportion of the total data
A greater proportion of the total data

quiz.q: If a circle graph shows that 50% of students prefer summer, what fraction of the circle would represent summer?
options: A fraction unrelated to the data

quiz.q: Why might someone use a circle graph instead of a bar graph?
options: A reason unrelated to graphing data
To show how parts relate to the whole as proportions
Bar graphs and circle graphs always show identical information
Circle graphs cannot display any type of data

quiz.q: All of the slices in a circle graph should add up to ___.
options: A percentage unrelated to circle graphs

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Space: The Sun and Its Role in Our Solar System
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: the Sun is a star at the centre of our solar system, providing the light and heat energy that supports life on Earth.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: The Sun is best described as a ___.
options: Star at the centre of our solar system

quiz.q: The Sun provides Earth with ___.
options: A concept unrelated to the Sun

quiz.q: Why is the Sun important for life on Earth?
options: The Sun has no connection to life on Earth
It provides the energy that plants and other living things depend on
Life on Earth exists with no need for any energy source
A reason unrelated to the Sun’s role

quiz.q: All the planets in our solar system orbit ___.
options: A body unrelated to our solar system

quiz.q: Compared to the planets, the Sun is ___.
options: Exactly the same size as Earth
Much smaller and reflects light from Earth
A description unrelated to the Sun
Far larger and is a source of its own light

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Comparing Urban, Suburban, and Rural Communities
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: communities can be classified as urban (city), suburban (residential areas near a city), or rural (countryside), each with different features and ways of life.

quiz.q: An urban community is best described as ___.
options: An area with very few people and mostly farmland
A community found only in the far north

quiz.q: A rural community is best described as ___.
options: A countryside area often used for farming, with a lower population
An area with no land used for any purpose

quiz.q: A suburban community is best described as ___.
options: The very centre of a large city
An area used only for large-scale farming
A residential area located near, but outside, a city’s downtown

quiz.q: Which of these might you expect to find more often in a rural community?
options: A feature unrelated to rural communities

quiz.q: Why is it useful to compare urban, suburban, and rural communities?
options: These types of communities have no differences worth studying
It helps show how geography and population shape different ways of life
All communities in Canada are identical
A reason unrelated to social studies

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Using Graphic Organizers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: graphic organizers, such as Venn diagrams and story maps, help readers visually organize information and ideas from a text.

quiz.q: A graphic organizer helps readers ___.
options: Visually organize information and ideas from a text
Memorize a text word for word
Avoid reading the text at all

quiz.q: A Venn diagram is often used to show ___.
options: A concept unrelated to graphic organizers
Similarities and differences between two things
The order of events in a single story only
The page numbers of a book

quiz.q: A story map is often used to organize ___.
options: A concept unrelated to graphic organizers
The characters, setting, problem, and events of a story
Only the title of a book

quiz.q: Why might a reader use a graphic organizer before writing a summary?
options: Graphic organizers never help with understanding a text
It replaces the need to read the text at all
It helps sort out the most important ideas and details first
A reason unrelated to reading strategies

quiz.q: Which graphic organizer would best help compare two book characters?
options: A concept unrelated to graphic organizers

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Mean, Median, and Mode (Intro)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: the mean is the average of a set of numbers, the median is the middle value when numbers are ordered, and the mode is the value that appears most often.

quiz.q: The mean of a set of numbers is found by ___.
options: Choosing the largest number in the set
Adding all the numbers and dividing by how many there are
A method unrelated to finding the mean
Choosing the smallest number in the set

quiz.q: The median of a set of numbers is ___.
options: The sum of all the numbers
A value unrelated to the median
The middle value when the numbers are placed in order

quiz.q: The mode of a set of numbers is ___.
options: The value that appears most often
A value unrelated to the mode
The average of all the values
The largest value in the set

quiz.q: What is the median of the set 2, 4, 6, 8, 10?
options: A value unrelated to the median

quiz.q: What is the mode of the set 3, 5, 5, 7, 9?
options: A value unrelated to the mode

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Energy Transformations in Everyday Devices
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: many everyday devices transform energy from one form to another, such as a toaster changing electrical energy into heat energy.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Energy transformation means energy ___.
options: Stays in the exact same form forever
Changes from one form into another

quiz.q: A toaster transforms electrical energy into ___.
options: A form of energy unrelated to toasters

quiz.q: A light bulb transforms electrical energy into ___.
options: A form of energy unrelated to light bulbs

quiz.q: A speaker transforms electrical energy into ___.
options: A form of energy unrelated to speakers

quiz.q: Why is it useful to understand energy transformations in devices?
options: It helps explain how everyday technology works and uses energy
Devices never use or change energy in any way
Energy transformations have no connection to how devices work

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Cultural Diversity and Multiculturalism in Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: Canada is home to people from many different cultural backgrounds, and multiculturalism celebrates and protects this diversity as an official policy.

quiz.q: Multiculturalism is a policy that ___.
options: Requires everyone to share the exact same culture
Ignores the cultural backgrounds of Canadians
A concept unrelated to Canadian society
Celebrates and protects Canada’s cultural diversity

quiz.q: Cultural diversity in a community means ___.
options: No cultural traditions are practiced at all
Everyone in the community shares an identical background
People from many different cultural backgrounds live together

quiz.q: Which is an example of cultural diversity in a Canadian community?
options: Neighbours celebrating different cultural festivals throughout the year
A concept unrelated to cultural diversity
A town with no cultural traditions of any kind
A community where only one culture is ever represented

quiz.q: Why does Canada value multiculturalism?
options: Canada requires all citizens to abandon their cultural traditions
Multiculturalism provides no value to Canadian society
It recognizes and respects the contributions of people from many backgrounds
A reason unrelated to Canadian identity

quiz.q: How might multiculturalism be reflected in a Canadian city?
options: Through a complete absence of cultural variety
Through diverse foods, festivals, languages, and traditions

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Capitalization Rules
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: capitalization rules include starting every sentence with a capital letter, capitalizing proper nouns, and capitalizing the pronoun “I.”

quiz.q: Every sentence should begin with ___.
options: A number, with no letters at all

quiz.q: Proper nouns, such as names of people and places, should be ___.
options: A concept unrelated to proper nouns

quiz.q: The pronoun “I” should always be ___.
options: Capitalized, no matter where it appears in a sentence
Written in lowercase at the start of a sentence
Left out of formal writing entirely

quiz.q: Which sentence uses capitalization correctly?
options: My friend and I visited Toronto last summer.
my friend and i visited toronto last summer.
My friend and i visited Toronto last Summer.

quiz.q: Why are capitalization rules important in writing?
options: They help readers understand sentence boundaries and identify proper nouns
Capitalization has no effect on how writing is understood
Capital letters are only used to make writing look nicer

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Fractions, Decimals, and Patterns
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 51-60, including multiplying fractions, angles, ratios, transformations, and mean/median/mode.

quiz.q: Multiplying a fraction by a whole number is the same as ___.
options: Subtracting the fraction from the whole number
Adding that fraction the given number of times
Dividing the whole number by the fraction

quiz.q: A protractor is used to measure ___.
A ratio is used to ___.
options: Measure the volume of a container
Round a number to the nearest ten

quiz.q: A translation moves a shape by ___.
options: Turning it around a fixed point
Flipping it like a mirror image
Sliding it without flipping or turning it

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review fractions, angles, ratios, and data together?
options: Review never helps strengthen understanding of a subject
These topics have no connection to each other
A reason unrelated to reviewing math
It reinforces how these math concepts connect and build on one another

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Forces, Structures, and Systems
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 51-60, including ecosystems, rock classification, sound, circuits, balanced forces, states of matter, and energy transformations.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Interdependence in an ecosystem means living things ___.
options: Live completely apart from their environment
Rely on each other and their environment to survive
Never interact with each other in any way

quiz.q: Balanced forces on an object result in ___.
options: No change in the object’s motion

quiz.q: In a series circuit, components are connected ___.
options: With no connection between them at all
Along a single loop, one after another

quiz.q: Energy transformation means energy ___.
options: Changes from one form into another
Stays in the exact same form forever

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review forces, circuits, and energy together?
options: Each topic must be studied with no connection to the others
These topics have no connection to each other
Review is never useful in science
It reinforces how these interconnected science concepts relate to one another

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Culminating Task: Designing a Model Community
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: this culminating task asks students to apply Days 51-60 learning about mapping, government, industries, settlement, and diversity to design a model community.

quiz.q: Why might a student consider access to fresh water when designing a model community?
options: Communities have historically settled near water for drinking, transportation, and farming
A reason unrelated to settlement patterns
Model communities never need to consider natural resources
Fresh water has no connection to where communities are located

quiz.q: Why might a student include all three levels of government when planning a model community?
options: Levels of government have no connection to community planning
Different levels of government are each responsible for different community needs
A community only ever needs a single level of government
A reason unrelated to Canadian government structure

quiz.q: Why might a student choose to include diverse cultural festivals in a model community?
options: It reflects the value of cultural diversity and multiculturalism seen in real Canadian communities
Model communities should avoid representing any culture at all
Cultural diversity has no relevance to designing a community

quiz.q: Why might a student include local industries, such as farming or manufacturing, in a model community?
options: Industries reflect how a community’s geography supports its economy
Every community relies on exactly the same industry
Industries have no connection to how a community functions

quiz.q: Why is a culminating task a valuable way to end this set of Social Studies lessons?
options: Culminating tasks never help reinforce prior learning
A reason unrelated to social studies learning
Applying multiple ideas together provides no educational benefit
It lets students apply many ideas from the unit together in one project

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Analogies
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: an analogy compares two pairs of words that share the same kind of relationship, such as hot is to cold as up is to down.

quiz.q: An analogy compares two pairs of words that share the same ___.
Which word completes the analogy: Bird is to nest as bee is to ___?
Which word completes the analogy: Puppy is to dog as kitten is to ___?
Hot is to cold as up is to ___?
Why are analogies useful for building vocabulary?
options: Analogies have no connection to word meaning
They help readers see how word meanings and relationships connect
Analogies only test spelling, not meaning

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Place Value to 10,000
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: place value to 10,000 describes the value of each digit in a number based on its position -- ones, tens, hundreds, and thousands.

quiz.q: In the number 6,482, the digit 4 represents ___.
What is the value of the 7 in 7,315?
Which number has a 5 in the thousands place?
What is 3,000 + 400 + 20 + 6 written as a standard number?
Why is it useful to understand place value in large numbers?
options: It helps read, write, and compare numbers accurately
Every digit in a number always has the same value
Place value has no effect on the meaning of a number

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Canadian Biomes: Forests, Wetlands, and Tundra
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: Canada contains several distinct biomes, including forests, wetlands, and tundra, each with its own climate, plants, and animals.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A biome is best described as a large region with a distinct ___.
Which biome is found in Canada’s far north, with very cold temperatures and few trees?
A wetland is an area that is ___.
options: Covered by water for at least part of the year

quiz.q: Which biome covers much of central and northern Ontario, with many coniferous and deciduous trees?
Why do scientists study different biomes?
options: To understand how climate shapes the plants and animals living there
Every biome on Earth is exactly the same
Biomes provide no useful scientific information

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Maya Civilization
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: the ancient Maya built cities in Central America between about 2000 BCE and 1500 CE, known for their pyramids, writing system, and knowledge of astronomy.

quiz.q: The ancient Maya civilization was located in ___.
The Maya built large stone structures known as ___.
The Maya developed a system of ___ to record dates and events.
The Maya were skilled observers of ___.
Why do historians study the ruins of Maya cities today?
options: Maya ruins provide no historical information
The Maya left behind no evidence of their civilization
They reveal how the Maya lived, built, and organized their society

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Alliteration
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: alliteration is the repetition of the same beginning consonant sound in a series of nearby words, such as slippery snakes slither silently.

quiz.q: Alliteration is the repetition of the same ___ in nearby words.
Which phrase is an example of alliteration?
Which phrase is an example of alliteration?
options: The cat sat on the mat

quiz.q: Why might a poet use alliteration?
options: Alliteration has no effect on how a poem sounds
To create rhythm and make language more memorable
To make a poem harder to read aloud

quiz.q: Which word best continues this alliteration pattern: Silly snakes slid ___?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplying 2-Digit by 2-Digit Numbers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: multiplying a 2-digit number by another 2-digit number involves breaking each factor into tens and ones, then adding the partial products together.

quiz.q: When multiplying 2-digit numbers, each factor can be broken into ___.
What is 12 × 13?
What is 21 × 14?
What is 15 × 15?
Why is it helpful to break numbers into tens and ones before multiplying?
options: It makes multiplying large numbers more manageable in smaller steps
It changes the value of the original numbers
Breaking numbers apart makes multiplication impossible

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Animal Camouflage and Mimicry
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: camouflage helps an animal blend into its surroundings, while mimicry occurs when one species evolves to resemble another species, both as strategies for survival.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Camouflage helps an animal ___.
options: Blend into its surroundings to avoid predators
Attract as much attention as possible

quiz.q: Mimicry occurs when one species ___.
options: Has no connection to any other species
Resembles another species for protection or advantage

quiz.q: Which is an example of camouflage?
options: A polar bear’s white fur blending into snow
A peacock’s bright, colourful tail feathers

quiz.q: Why might a harmless insect evolve to look like a stinging wasp?
options: To trick predators into avoiding it, an example of mimicry
Looking similar to another species provides no benefit

quiz.q: How do camouflage and mimicry both help animals survive?
options: They make animals more visible to predators
X unrelated to animal survival strategies
They have no effect on an animal’s chances of survival
They help animals avoid predators or better catch prey

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Nubia (Kingdom of Kush)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: ancient Nubia, home to the Kingdom of Kush, was located along the Nile River south of Egypt and became known for its skilled ironworkers, pyramids, and trade in gold.

quiz.q: The ancient Kingdom of Kush was located ___.
options: Along the Nile River, south of Egypt

quiz.q: Nubia became especially well known for its skill in working with ___.
Like ancient Egypt, the Kingdom of Kush built ___.
Nubia traded valuable resources such as ___ with neighbouring regions.
Why is it important to learn about ancient Nubia alongside ancient Egypt?
options: Only Egypt existed in ancient Africa
X unrelated to ancient African civilizations
It shows another powerful African civilization with its own rich history
Nubia had no history worth studying

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Interjections
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: an interjection is a word or phrase that expresses strong emotion, such as Wow! or Ouch!, and is often followed by an exclamation mark.

quiz.q: An interjection is a word that expresses ___.
Which word is an interjection?
An interjection is often followed by ___.
Which sentence contains an interjection?
options: The dog ran across the yard.

quiz.q: Why might a writer use an interjection in dialogue?
options: To replace the need for any punctuation
To show a character’s strong feeling or reaction

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Rounding Decimals
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: rounding decimals means adjusting a decimal number to a nearby, simpler value, such as rounding to the nearest whole number or nearest tenth.

quiz.q: Rounding a decimal means adjusting it to a nearby, simpler ___.
What is 4.7 rounded to the nearest whole number?
What is 2.3 rounded to the nearest whole number?
What is 6.85 rounded to the nearest tenth?
Why might someone round a decimal number in everyday life?
options: Rounding decimals serves no practical purpose
To make an estimate or simplify a number quickly
Rounding always makes a number less accurate for every purpose

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Space: Earth’s Rotation and the Day/Night Cycle
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: Earth rotates on its axis once approximately every 24 hours, and this rotation causes the cycle of day and night.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Earth’s rotation on its axis takes approximately ___.
Day and night occur because Earth ___.
options: X unrelated to day and night

quiz.q: When your part of Earth is facing the Sun, it is ___.
Which term describes Earth spinning on its axis?
Why do different places on Earth experience day and night at different times?
options: The entire Earth always faces the Sun at once
The Sun turns on and off each day
Only half of the rotating Earth faces the Sun at any moment

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Tools and Technology
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: early societies developed tools and technology, such as farming implements, pottery, and metalworking, to meet their daily needs and improve their way of life.

quiz.q: Early societies developed tools mainly to ___.
options: Meet daily needs, such as farming and building
Avoid doing any work at all

quiz.q: Which material did many early societies learn to shape into tools and weapons?
options: Metal, such as bronze or iron

quiz.q: Pottery was an important early technology used mainly for ___.
options: Storing and carrying food and water

quiz.q: How did farming tools, such as the plow, change early societies?
options: They allowed people to grow more food and support larger communities
Farming tools had no effect on early societies

quiz.q: Why do historians study the tools used by early societies?
options: Studying tools provides no historical information
Early societies never used any tools
Tools reveal how people lived, worked, and solved problems

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Identifying Theme
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: the theme of a story is its central message or lesson about life, which is different from the plot, or the events that happen.

quiz.q: The theme of a story is its ___.
options: Central message or lesson about life

quiz.q: How is theme different from plot?
options: Theme is the underlying message, while plot is the sequence of events
Plot is the message and theme is the setting
Theme and plot always mean exactly the same thing

quiz.q: A story about a character who learns to be honest after telling a lie likely has a theme about ___.
options: X unrelated to the story’s theme

quiz.q: Why might two different stories share the same theme, such as courage?
options: Different plots and characters can still teach a similar life lesson
Every story must have a completely unique theme
Themes can never repeat across different stories

quiz.q: Which question would best help a reader identify a story’s theme?
options: What colour is the book’s cover?
What lesson does this story teach about life?
How many pages does this story have?

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Estimating Products and Quotients
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: estimating products and quotients involves rounding numbers before multiplying or dividing to quickly predict a reasonable answer.

quiz.q: Estimating a product or quotient means ___.
options: Rounding numbers first to quickly predict a reasonable answer
Calculating the exact answer with no rounding

quiz.q: Which is the best estimate for 29 × 4?
Which is the best estimate for 58 ÷ 6?
To estimate 19 × 21, you might round to ___.
options: X unrelated to estimating this product

quiz.q: Why is estimating useful before solving a multiplication or division problem?
options: Estimating never helps with checking an answer
It helps check whether the exact answer is reasonable
Estimating always gives the exact same result as calculating

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Matter: Why Objects Float or Sink (Buoyancy Basics)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: whether an object floats or sinks in water depends on its buoyancy, which compares the object’s weight to the weight of the water it displaces.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Buoyancy refers to the ability of an object to ___.
options: Float in a liquid such as water

quiz.q: An object generally sinks in water when it is ___.
options: Denser (heavier for its size) than water
Lighter for its size than water
Exactly the same temperature as water

quiz.q: Which of these would most likely float in water?
Why can a large steel ship float, even though steel is denser than water?
options: Its hollow shape displaces enough water to support its weight
Steel always sinks with no exceptions
Ships are not actually made of steel

quiz.q: Why is understanding buoyancy useful in everyday life?
options: All objects behave identically in water
It helps explain how boats, life jackets, and other objects behave in water

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Leadership and Government Structures
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: early societies organized leadership in different ways, from kings and pharaohs to councils of elders, to make decisions and maintain order.

quiz.q: Many early societies were led by a single powerful ruler, such as a ___.
Some early societies made decisions through a ___.
options: Coin toss with no discussion at all
Group of strangers chosen at random
Council of elders or respected leaders

quiz.q: Why did early societies need some form of leadership or government?
options: X unrelated to early government structures
Early societies never needed any organization
To make decisions, resolve conflicts, and maintain order
Leadership served no purpose in early societies

quiz.q: In ancient Egypt, the pharaoh was believed to be ___.
options: A god-like ruler with great authority
Chosen by a public vote every year
An ordinary citizen with no power

quiz.q: How did leadership structures in early societies differ from one another?
options: Early societies had no leaders at all
Every early society used the exact same system of leadership
X unrelated to comparing early societies
Some had a single ruler, while others relied on councils or shared leadership

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Writing: Newspaper Report Writing
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a newspaper report presents factual information about a recent event, answering the questions who, what, where, when, why, and how in a clear headline and opening paragraph.

quiz.q: A newspaper report is mainly meant to ___.
options: Share a completely fictional made-up story
Present factual information about a recent event
Persuade readers to buy a product

quiz.q: A strong newspaper report usually answers ___.
options: Who, what, where, when, why, and how

quiz.q: The headline of a newspaper report should ___.
options: Summarize the story in a short, attention-grabbing way
Be longer than the entire article
Never relate to the story’s content

quiz.q: Where is the most important information usually placed in a newspaper report?
options: Near the beginning, in the opening paragraph
Only at the very end of the article

quiz.q: Why should a newspaper report stick to facts rather than opinions?
options: Opinions are always more important than facts
Facts are never important in a newspaper report
So readers can trust the information as accurate and unbiased

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Classifying Polygons by Sides and Angles
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: polygons are classified by their number of sides and angles, and can be described as regular (equal sides and angles) or irregular (unequal sides and angles).

quiz.q: A polygon is classified based on its number of ___.
A polygon with five sides is called a ___.
A polygon with six sides is called a ___.
A regular polygon has ___.
options: A different number of sides each time

quiz.q: Which best describes an irregular polygon?
options: It must always be a circle
It has no sides or angles at all
Its sides and angles are not all equal

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Weather: Cloud Types and What They Predict
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: clouds form in different shapes and heights, and identifying cloud types, such as cumulus, stratus, and cirrus, can help predict upcoming weather.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Clouds form when water vapour in the air ___.
options: Cools and condenses into tiny droplets

quiz.q: Fluffy, puffy clouds with flat bottoms are called ___ clouds.
Thin, wispy clouds high in the sky are called ___ clouds.
Flat, grey clouds that cover the sky like a blanket are called ___ clouds.
Why might meteorologists study cloud types?
options: Cloud shapes and heights can help predict upcoming weather
Cloud types provide no useful weather information
All clouds always predict the exact same weather

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Canadian Shield: Formation and Landscape
