subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: the Canadian Shield is a vast region of ancient rock covering much of central and northern Canada, shaped by glaciers and known for its lakes, forests, and mineral deposits.

quiz.q: The Canadian Shield is best described as a vast region of ___.
options: Flat farmland found only in southern Ontario
Ancient rock covering much of central and northern Canada
X unrelated to the Canadian Shield

quiz.q: The landscape of the Canadian Shield was heavily shaped by ___.
options: Ocean waves along a tropical coastline
Glaciers moving across the land long ago
X unrelated to the Canadian Shield’s formation

quiz.q: The Canadian Shield is known for having many ___.
options: X unrelated to the Canadian Shield

quiz.q: Why is the rock of the Canadian Shield considered especially old?
options: It formed billions of years ago, making it some of the oldest rock on Earth
X unrelated to the Canadian Shield
The rock is not actually old at all
It formed only a few years ago

quiz.q: Which of these regions of Canada would you find part of the Canadian Shield?
options: X unrelated to the Canadian Shield’s location

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Spelling: Silent Letters
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: some English words contain silent letters that are written but not pronounced, such as the k in knee or the b in comb.

quiz.q: A silent letter is a letter that is ___.
Which letter is silent in the word knee?
Which letter is silent in the word comb?
Which letter is silent in the word write?
Why can silent letters make spelling tricky for readers?
options: Silent letters are always pronounced exactly as written
A letter appears in the word but is not heard when the word is spoken
Silent letters never appear in the English language

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Measuring Capacity: Litres and Millilitres
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: capacity describes how much liquid a container can hold, and is measured in millilitres (mL) for small amounts and litres (L) for larger amounts.

quiz.q: Capacity describes how much ___ a container can hold.
Which unit would best measure the capacity of a large water bottle?
Which unit would best measure a small spoonful of liquid medicine?
How many millilitres are in 1 litre?
Why is it useful to know both litres and millilitres?
options: Litres and millilitres always measure the exact same amount
Litres suit larger amounts, while millilitres suit smaller, more precise amounts
Only one of these units is ever used in real life

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Forces: Air Resistance and Motion
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: air resistance is a force that pushes against objects as they move through the air, slowing them down depending on their shape and speed.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Air resistance is a force that ___.
options: Pushes against an object moving through the air
Pulls every object straight down to the ground

quiz.q: Which object would experience more air resistance while falling?
options: A tightly folded piece of paper

quiz.q: Why does a flat sheet of paper fall more slowly than a crumpled ball of the same paper?
options: Flat paper weighs much more than crumpled paper
Air resistance has no effect on falling paper
Its larger surface area creates more air resistance

quiz.q: How does air resistance affect a moving bicycle?
options: It has no effect on how a bicycle moves
It always speeds up the bicycle
It pushes against the rider, making it harder to pedal quickly

quiz.q: Why might race car designers shape cars to reduce air resistance?
options: Race cars are designed to increase air resistance as much as possible
A streamlined shape lets the car move faster with less resistance
Reducing air resistance has no benefit for a moving car

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s National Parks and Protected Areas
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: Canada has established national parks and other protected areas to preserve natural landscapes, wildlife, and ecosystems for future generations.

quiz.q: A national park is an area of land that is ___.
options: Protected to preserve its natural landscape and wildlife
Open for unlimited building and development

quiz.q: Why does Canada create protected areas such as national parks?
options: To ensure land is developed as quickly as possible
To conserve natural environments and wildlife for future generations
Protected areas serve no environmental purpose

quiz.q: Which of these activities is commonly allowed in many Canadian national parks?
options: X unrelated to national park activities

quiz.q: Banff National Park, one of Canada’s oldest national parks, is located in ___.
How do national parks help protect biodiversity?
options: National parks have no connection to biodiversity
They preserve habitats where many plants and animals can continue to live
National parks remove all wildlife from an area
X unrelated to protecting the environment

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Direct Objects
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a direct object is the noun or pronoun that receives the action of a verb in a sentence, answering the question what or whom.

quiz.q: A direct object is the part of a sentence that ___.
options: Performs the action of the verb
Describes the setting of a sentence
Receives the action of the verb

quiz.q: In the sentence Maya kicked the ball, what is the direct object?
options: X unrelated to the direct object

quiz.q: In the sentence The chef baked a cake, what is the direct object?
options: X unrelated to the direct object

quiz.q: A direct object answers which question about the verb?
In the sentence The dog chased the squirrel, what is the direct object?
options: X unrelated to the direct object

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing Prices: Unit Rate and Best Value
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: comparing prices using unit rate means finding the cost of a single item or unit, which helps shoppers decide which option offers the best value.

quiz.q: A unit rate compares the cost of ___.
options: Only the tax on a purchase

quiz.q: If 4 apples cost 2 dollars, what is the unit rate per apple?
options: X unrelated to this unit rate

quiz.q: A pack of 5 pencils costs 5 dollars, and a pack of 10 pencils costs 8 dollars. Which has the better unit rate?
options: X unrelated to comparing unit rates
Both packs have the exact same unit rate

quiz.q: Why is finding the unit rate helpful when shopping?
options: Unit rate makes it impossible to compare prices
It helps shoppers compare prices fairly to find the best value
The total price is always more useful than the unit rate

quiz.q: If 3 juice boxes cost 6 dollars, what is the cost per juice box?
options: X unrelated to this unit rate

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Structures: Why Shape Affects Strength (Triangles vs Squares)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: the shape of a structure affects its strength, and triangles are especially strong because their fixed angles resist bending and twisting better than squares.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Triangles are considered a strong shape in structures because their angles ___.
options: Change shape very easily under pressure
Have no effect on a structure’s strength
Resist bending and twisting under pressure

quiz.q: Compared to a triangle, a square shape is more likely to ___.
options: Collapse or change shape under pressure
Support far more weight than a triangle
Remain perfectly rigid under any pressure

quiz.q: Why do builders often use triangular supports in bridges and towers?
options: Triangular supports have no effect on strength
Triangles make a structure weaker and less stable
Triangles distribute force evenly and resist bending

quiz.q: If you push on one corner of a square frame, what is likely to happen?
options: The square remains rigid and never changes shape
The square becomes a triangle automatically
X unrelated to testing shape strength
The square can shift into a different shape, such as a parallelogram

quiz.q: Why might engineers test different shapes before building a structure?
options: Testing shapes provides no useful information for engineers
All shapes provide exactly the same strength
To find the shape that provides the most strength and stability

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Time Zones Across Canada
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: because Canada spans such a wide area from east to west, it is divided into six time zones, meaning it can be a different time of day in different parts of the country at once.

quiz.q: Canada is divided into time zones because it ___.
options: Has only one region with people living in it
Spans a very wide area from east to west
X unrelated to Canada’s time zones

quiz.q: How many time zones does Canada have?
options: X unrelated to Canada’s time zones

quiz.q: If it is 3:00 p.m. in Ontario (Eastern time), it is generally earlier in the day in ___.
options: A place in exactly the same time zone
X unrelated to comparing time zones

quiz.q: Why might someone need to consider time zones when calling a friend in another province?
options: Every province in Canada shares the exact same time
Time zones only matter for international travel
The time of day may be different in each province

quiz.q: Time zones are generally organized based on a location’s ___.

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Skimming and Scanning for Information
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: skimming means quickly reading a text to get its general idea, while scanning means searching a text quickly for specific facts, such as a date or name.

quiz.q: Skimming a text means reading quickly to ___.
options: Get the general idea of the text

quiz.q: Scanning a text means searching quickly for ___.
options: The entire meaning of the text

quiz.q: Which strategy would be best for quickly finding a specific date in a long article?
options: X unrelated to finding a specific fact

quiz.q: Which strategy would be best for getting a quick overview of what a chapter is about?
options: Memorizing the chapter word for word
X unrelated to getting an overview

quiz.q: Why are skimming and scanning useful reading strategies?
options: They help readers find information efficiently without reading every word
These strategies always take longer than reading word for word
They prevent readers from understanding a text at all

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Tally Charts and Frequency Tables
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: a tally chart uses marks to record how often something occurs, and this data can be organized into a frequency table showing the total count for each category.

quiz.q: A tally chart uses marks to record ___.
options: The exact size of an object

quiz.q: In tally marks, a group of five is usually shown as ___.
options: Four vertical lines with a line crossed through them

quiz.q: A frequency table organizes data by showing the ___ for each category.
If a tally chart shows a group of 5, then a group of 5, then 2 more marks for a category, how many are recorded?
options: X unrelated to this tally count

quiz.q: Why might a survey use a tally chart while collecting data?
options: Tally charts make it impossible to count responses
Tally charts can only be used for numbers larger than 100
It makes it quick and easy to record and count responses

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Electricity: Reading Simple Circuit Diagrams
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: a circuit diagram uses standard symbols to represent parts of an electric circuit, such as a battery, switch, and light bulb, showing how they are connected.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A circuit diagram uses symbols to represent ___.
options: The colours of the wires only
The parts of an electric circuit and how they connect

quiz.q: Which symbol typically represents a battery in a circuit diagram?
options: A perfect circle with no lines
A pair of parallel lines, one longer than the other

quiz.q: In a circuit diagram, a switch symbol shows where the circuit can be ___.
options: Opened or closed to control the flow of electricity
Permanently broken with no way to reconnect it

quiz.q: Why are standard symbols used in circuit diagrams instead of drawings of real objects?
options: Standard symbols make circuits impossible to understand
Every country uses completely different, unrelated symbols
They allow anyone to read and understand a circuit clearly and quickly

quiz.q: If a circuit diagram shows a break in the line connecting the parts, what does this likely mean?
options: The circuit is complete and functioning normally
X unrelated to reading circuit diagrams
The circuit is open and electricity cannot flow
The diagram contains no useful information

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Natural Hazards in Canada (Floods, Forest Fires, Earthquakes)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: different regions of Canada face different natural hazards, such as floods near rivers, forest fires in dry forested areas, and earthquakes along the west coast.

quiz.q: A natural hazard is an event caused by nature that can ___.
options: Never affect people in any way
Threaten people, property, or the environment

quiz.q: Areas near rivers are especially at risk of ___.
Dry, forested regions of Canada are especially at risk of ___.
Which region of Canada is more likely to experience earthquakes?
options: The west coast, such as British Columbia

quiz.q: Why is it useful for communities to understand the natural hazards in their region?
options: Natural hazards never affect any Canadian community
Understanding natural hazards provides no benefit to a community
It helps them prepare and respond safely to potential emergencies

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Connotation and Denotation
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: denotation is the exact dictionary meaning of a word, while connotation is the feeling or association a word carries beyond its literal meaning.

quiz.q: Denotation refers to a word’s ___.
Connotation refers to the ___ a word carries beyond its literal meaning.
The words skinny and slim have similar denotations but different ___.
options: X unrelated to comparing these words

quiz.q: Which word has a more positive connotation than stubborn?
Why might a writer carefully choose words based on their connotation?
options: To create a specific feeling or impression for the reader
Connotation has no effect on how a reader feels
Every word carries exactly the same feeling

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Number Lines: Plotting Fractions and Decimals
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: fractions and decimals can be plotted on a number line to compare their values and see how they relate to whole numbers.

quiz.q: A number line can be used to ___.
options: Only show whole numbers greater than 100
Measure the weight of an object
Compare the values of fractions and decimals

quiz.q: On a number line from 0 to 1, where would 1/2 be plotted?
On a number line, which is closer to 1: 0.75 or 0.25?
options: X unrelated to comparing these decimals

quiz.q: Where would the fraction 3/4 be plotted on a number line between 0 and 1?
options: X unrelated to plotting this fraction

quiz.q: Why is it useful to plot fractions and decimals on the same number line?
options: It helps show how fractions and decimals relate to and compare with each other
Fractions and decimals can never be compared to one another
A number line can only show whole numbers

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Compound Machines: Combining Simple Machines
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: a compound machine is made by combining two or more simple machines, such as a wheelbarrow (lever and wheel and axle) or scissors (two levers), to make work easier.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A compound machine is made by combining ___.
options: A single simple machine used alone

quiz.q: A wheelbarrow is an example of a compound machine because it combines a ___.
options: X unrelated to how a wheelbarrow works
Wedge and an inclined plane only
Lever and a wheel and axle

quiz.q: Scissors are considered a compound machine because they are made of ___.
options: X unrelated to how scissors work

quiz.q: Why might combining simple machines create a more useful tool?
options: Combined machines can make certain tasks even easier than a single simple machine
Combining simple machines always makes tasks harder to complete
Simple machines cannot be combined with one another

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of a compound machine?
options: A single ramp with nothing else attached
A bicycle, which combines wheels, levers, and gears
A single lever with no other parts

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Canada’s Population Distribution: Where People Live
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: most of Canada’s population lives in a narrow band near the southern border, close to resources, transportation, and warmer climates, while northern regions remain sparsely populated.

quiz.q: Most of Canada’s population lives ___.
options: Spread evenly across the entire country
In a narrow band near the southern border

quiz.q: Why do fewer people live in Canada’s northern regions?
options: The climate is harsher and there are fewer transportation routes and services
Everyone in Canada is required to live in the south
The north has no land at all

quiz.q: Which factor most influences where Canadians choose to settle?
options: Access to resources, jobs, and transportation
The colour of the provincial flag
The number of letters in a city’s name

quiz.q: Which of these Canadian cities would likely have one of the largest populations?
options: X unrelated to comparing population size

quiz.q: Why do geographers study population distribution?
options: Every region of Canada has an identical population
Population distribution provides no useful information
It helps explain how and why people are spread across a country

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Review: Vocabulary, Grammar, and Reading Skills (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including analogies, alliteration, interjections, theme, and skimming and scanning.

quiz.q: An analogy compares two pairs of words that share the same ___.
Alliteration is the repetition of the same ___ in nearby words.
An interjection is a word that expresses ___.
The theme of a story is its ___.
options: Central message or lesson about life

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review vocabulary, grammar, and reading strategies together?
options: Reviewing past learning never helps strengthen understanding
It reinforces how these language skills support clear reading and writing
X unrelated to reviewing language arts
These skills have no connection to each other

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Review: Number Sense, Geometry, and Data (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including place value, multiplying 2-digit numbers, rounding decimals, classifying polygons, and tally charts.

quiz.q: In the number 6,482, the digit 4 represents ___.
What is 12 × 13?
What is 4.7 rounded to the nearest whole number?
A polygon with five sides is called a ___.
Why is it useful to review place value, multiplication, and geometry together?
options: These topics have no connection to each other
Review never helps strengthen understanding of a subject
It reinforces how these math concepts connect and build on one another

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Review: Ecosystems, Earth and Space, and Structures (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including Canadian biomes, camouflage, Earth’s rotation, buoyancy, air resistance, and structural strength.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A biome is best described as a large region with a distinct ___.
Camouflage helps an animal ___.
options: Blend into its surroundings to avoid predators
Attract as much attention as possible

quiz.q: Day and night occur because Earth ___.
options: X unrelated to day and night

quiz.q: Triangles are considered a strong shape in structures because their angles ___.
options: Have no effect on a structure’s strength
Change shape very easily under pressure
Resist bending and twisting under pressure

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review ecosystems, space, and structures together?
options: Each topic must be studied with no connection to the others
It reinforces how these science concepts each explain different parts of the natural world
These topics have no connection to each other
Review is never useful in science

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Review: Early Societies and Canadian Geography (Days 61-69)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: this review lesson revisits key ideas from Days 61-69, including the Maya, Nubia, early leadership, the Canadian Shield, time zones, and population distribution.

quiz.q: The ancient Maya civilization was located in ___.
Nubia became especially well known for its skill in working with ___.
The Canadian Shield is best described as a vast region of ___.
options: Flat farmland found only in southern Ontario
X unrelated to the Canadian Shield
Ancient rock covering much of central and northern Canada

quiz.q: How many time zones does Canada have?
options: X unrelated to Canada’s time zones

quiz.q: Why is it useful to review early societies alongside Canadian geography?
options: These topics have no connection to social studies
Early societies and Canadian geography are exactly the same topic
It helps compare how different societies and regions have developed over time
X unrelated to reviewing social studies

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Vocabulary: Proverbs and Adages
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a proverb or adage is a short, well-known saying that expresses a piece of traditional wisdom or advice, such as look before you leap.

quiz.q: A proverb or adage is best described as a short saying that expresses ___.
Which saying is an example of a proverb?
The proverb the early bird catches the worm suggests that ___.
options: Waiting is always the best choice
Birds only eat worms in the morning

quiz.q: Why do people still use proverbs that are hundreds of years old?
options: Their advice about life often still applies today
Proverbs are only used in ancient texts
Old sayings have no meaning today

quiz.q: Which proverb best warns against judging something only by its outward appearance?
options: Don’t judge a book by its cover
The early bird catches the worm

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Comparing and Ordering Numbers to 10,000
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: comparing and ordering numbers to 10,000 means using place value to decide whether one number is greater than, less than, or equal to another, and arranging numbers from least to greatest or greatest to least.

quiz.q: To compare two numbers, you should first look at the digit with the greatest ___.
Which symbol means greater than?
Which number is greater: 4,562 or 4,265?
Which of these lists correctly orders the numbers from least to greatest?
Why is comparing and ordering numbers a useful skill in everyday life?
options: Every number is always considered equal in size
It helps make sense of amounts, scores, or measurements
Comparing numbers serves no everyday purpose

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Freshwater Habitats: Ponds, Lakes, and Rivers
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: freshwater habitats, such as ponds, lakes, and rivers, contain low levels of salt and support plants and animals, such as frogs, fish, and cattails, that are adapted to living in fresh water.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A freshwater habitat contains water with ___.
Which of these is an example of a freshwater habitat?
Which plant commonly grows along the edges of ponds and marshes?
Rivers differ from lakes and ponds mainly because river water ___.
options: X unrelated to comparing freshwater habitats

quiz.q: Why are freshwater habitats important to many communities?
options: Freshwater habitats provide no benefit to communities
Only ocean habitats support living things
They provide drinking water and support diverse plants and animals

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Ancient Persia
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: the ancient Persian Empire, centred in what is now Iran, grew to become one of the largest empires in the ancient world, known for its royal roads, diverse cultures, and organized system of governing distant provinces.

quiz.q: The ancient Persian Empire was centred in the region that is now known as ___.
The Persian Empire became known for building an extensive network of ___.
options: Roads connecting distant parts of the empire

quiz.q: To govern such a large empire, Persian rulers divided their land into ___.
options: Provinces, each managed by an appointed governor

quiz.q: The Persian Empire is remembered for allowing conquered peoples to ___.
options: Live with no laws at all
Keep many of their own customs and beliefs

quiz.q: Why do historians consider ancient Persia to be one of the largest empires of the ancient world?
options: X unrelated to studying ancient Persia
Ancient Persia had no organized government
It controlled a vast amount of land and many diverse peoples
Persia never expanded beyond a single city

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Figurative Language: Onomatopoeia
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: onomatopoeia is a word that imitates the sound it describes, such as buzz, crash, or sizzle.

quiz.q: Onomatopoeia is a word that ___.
options: Has the opposite meaning of another word

quiz.q: Which word is an example of onomatopoeia?
Which word is an example of onomatopoeia?
Why might an author use onomatopoeia in a story?
options: Onomatopoeia has no effect on how a reader imagines a scene
To help readers imagine the sounds happening in the scene
To make a sentence longer with no other purpose

quiz.q: Which sentence uses onomatopoeia?
options: The bacon sizzled loudly in the pan
The bacon cooked in the pan
The pan was on the stove

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Multiplication Strategies: The Distributive Property
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: the distributive property is a multiplication strategy that breaks one factor into smaller parts, multiplies each part separately, and then adds the results together, such as 6 x 14 = (6 x 10) + (6 x 4).

quiz.q: The distributive property breaks a factor into ___ before multiplying.
options: X unrelated to the distributive property

quiz.q: Using the distributive property, 6 x 14 can be broken into ___.
What is 7 x 13 using the distributive property: (7 x 10) + (7 x 3)?
What is 5 x 24 using the distributive property: (5 x 20) + (5 x 4)?
Why is the distributive property a useful multiplication strategy?
options: It makes multiplication problems impossible to solve
It only works for adding two numbers together
It breaks a hard multiplication problem into smaller, simpler steps

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Animal Behaviours: Migration and Hibernation
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: some animals survive seasonal changes through migration, travelling long distances to a different climate, while others survive through hibernation, a long period of deep rest during which their body slows down.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Migration is best described as an animal ___.
options: Changing colour to match its surroundings
Sleeping through the entire winter without moving
Travelling long distances to reach a different climate

quiz.q: Hibernation is best described as a long period during which an animal’s body ___.
options: Grows a completely new set of fur
Slows down and enters a deep, resting state

quiz.q: Which of these animals is well known for migrating long distances each year?
Which of these animals is well known for hibernating through the winter?
Why might migration and hibernation both help animals survive cold winters?
options: X unrelated to animal survival strategies
They help animals avoid harsh conditions or a shortage of food
These behaviours make survival more difficult for animals
All animals behave the exact same way in winter

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: The Vikings: Explorers and Traders
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: the Vikings were seafaring people from Scandinavia who, between about 800 and 1050 CE, explored, traded, and settled across parts of Europe and even reached North America in their longships.

quiz.q: The Vikings originally came from the region known today as ___.
Vikings travelled across the seas using long, narrow boats known as ___.
Besides raiding, Vikings were also skilled ___.
options: Traders who exchanged goods across long distances

quiz.q: Evidence suggests Vikings reached which part of North America long before other Europeans?
options: A settlement in what is now Mexico
A settlement in what is now Newfoundland
A settlement in what is now Hawaii

quiz.q: Why are the Vikings remembered as skilled sailors?
options: X unrelated to Viking sailing skills
Vikings never travelled far from home
They travelled vast distances across open ocean using their longships

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Grammar: Compound and Complex Sentences
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a compound sentence joins two complete sentences with a joining word, such as and or but, while a complex sentence joins a complete sentence with an incomplete idea using a word such as because or although.

quiz.q: A compound sentence joins two complete sentences using a ___.
options: Joining word, such as and or but

quiz.q: Which sentence is a compound sentence?
options: I finished my homework, and I played outside

quiz.q: A complex sentence joins a complete sentence with an incomplete idea using a word such as ___.
Which sentence is a complex sentence?
options: We stayed inside and read books
Because it was raining, we stayed inside

quiz.q: Why might a writer combine short sentences into compound or complex sentences?
options: Short sentences can never be combined
Combining sentences always confuses the reader
To show how ideas connect and to make writing flow more smoothly

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Long Division: Dividing 3-Digit Numbers by a 1-Digit Divisor
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: dividing a 3-digit number by a 1-digit divisor uses the same long division steps as smaller problems -- divide, multiply, subtract, and bring down -- one digit at a time from left to right.

quiz.q: When dividing a 3-digit number by a 1-digit divisor, you work through the digits from ___.
options: Right to left, all at once
Left to right, one digit at a time

quiz.q: What is 936 divided by 3?
What is 848 divided by 4?
What is 639 divided by 3?
Why is it helpful to divide, multiply, subtract, and bring down one step at a time?
options: It makes the division problem impossible to complete
It breaks a large division problem into smaller, more manageable steps
X unrelated to long division steps
It changes the value of the original number being divided

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Fossils: Clues to Earth’s Past
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: a fossil is the preserved remains or trace of a plant or animal that lived long ago, and studying fossils helps scientists learn what life on Earth was like millions of years ago.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: A fossil is best described as the preserved ___ of a living thing from long ago.
Fossils are most commonly found in which type of rock?
options: X unrelated to where fossils form

quiz.q: Which of these could be preserved as a fossil?
options: A dinosaur’s footprint pressed into mud

quiz.q: Why do scientists study fossils?
options: Fossils provide no information about the past
To learn what plants, animals, and environments were like long ago
Fossils only reveal information about modern life

quiz.q: About how long can it take for a fossil to form?

subjects.subject: SocialStudies
subjects.title: Early Societies: Farming and Food Production
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Social Studies strand: early societies developed farming methods, such as planting crops and raising animals, that allowed them to produce a reliable food supply and settle in permanent communities.

quiz.q: Farming allowed early societies to produce a more reliable ___.
Before farming, many early people found food mainly by ___.
options: X unrelated to early food gathering
Hunting animals and gathering wild plants

quiz.q: How did farming change where early people chose to live?
options: X unrelated to early settlement patterns
It allowed them to settle in permanent communities near their fields
It forced people to move constantly with no fixed home
Farming had no effect on where people lived

quiz.q: Which of these is an example of an early farming activity?
options: Raising animals such as goats or cattle

quiz.q: Why is the development of farming considered such an important change in early societies?
options: Farming made it harder for communities to find food
Farming had no impact on how societies developed
It allowed populations to grow and communities to become more settled
X unrelated to the importance of early farming

subjects.subject: Language
subjects.title: Reading: Character Traits and Motivation
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Language strand: a character trait describes what a character is like, such as brave or curious, while motivation explains the reasons behind a character’s actions in a story.

quiz.q: A character trait describes ___.
options: The number of pages in a book

quiz.q: A character’s motivation explains ___.
options: The reasons behind a character’s actions
The colour of a character’s clothing

quiz.q: If a character shares their lunch with a hungry classmate, this shows the trait of ___.
options: X unrelated to this character trait

quiz.q: Why might a character in a story act bravely even when they are afraid?
options: Characters never have reasons for their actions
Fear always stops a character from acting
Their motivation, such as protecting a friend, drives them to act despite fear

quiz.q: How can a reader figure out a character’s traits?
options: X unrelated to identifying character traits
By paying attention to what the character says, does, and thinks
By only reading the book’s title
By ignoring the character’s actions completely

subjects.subject: Math
subjects.title: Fraction of a Set (Finding a Fractional Part of a Whole Number)
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Math strand: finding a fraction of a set means dividing a group of objects into equal parts and taking a certain number of those parts, such as finding 1/4 of 12 by dividing 12 into 4 equal groups.

quiz.q: To find a fraction of a set, you first divide the set into ___.
options: Equal parts based on the denominator
X unrelated to finding a fraction of a set

quiz.q: What is 1/4 of 12?
What is 1/3 of 15?
What is 2/5 of 20?
Why is it helpful to know how to find a fraction of a set?
options: It helps solve real-world problems, such as sharing a group of items equally
Fractions can only describe parts of a single whole object
X unrelated to fractions of a set
Finding a fraction of a set is never useful in real life

subjects.subject: Science
subjects.title: Simple Machines: Mechanical Advantage
subjects.summary: Grade 4 Science strand: mechanical advantage is the benefit a simple machine provides by making a task require less effort force, even though the distance or direction of the force may change.
subjects.resourceLabel: TVO Learn: Grade 4 Science and Technology

quiz.q: Mechanical advantage refers to how a simple machine ___.
options: Always makes a task take longer to complete
Removes the need for any force at all
Makes a task require less effort force

quiz.q: Using a lever to lift a heavy rock is easier because the lever ___.
options: X unrelated to how levers work
Provides mechanical advantage by reducing the effort needed
Has no effect on the force required
Makes the rock lighter in actual weight
